- [ ] `daemon-pre-pr-lint-gate` — daemon runs all in-tree CI lints locally before opening any PR; refuses to open if any fails
  - **ID**: daemon-pre-pr-lint-gate
  - **Tags**: p0, daemon, supervisor, throughput, self-improvement, pick-next
  - **Milestone**: M1
  - **Pick-next**: yes (operator-flagged 2026-05-05 — supervisor should pick this up immediately after its current task closes).
  - **Estimate**: 2d
  - **Hypothesis**: Empirical observation 2026-05-05 (88+ supervisor iterations, 3 shipped features): the daemon's first-pass output reliably fails CI lints I've already shipped (markdownlint MD001, rule-12 scope opt-out, rule-3 doc-first, rule-6 catch annotations, rule-7 chaos parsing). Each failure costs an operator-side babysitting commit. If the daemon runs `pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm vitest run && pnpm exec markdownlint-cli2 "**/*.md" && node scripts/check-rule-*.mjs` locally on its branch BEFORE calling `gh pr create`, every failure becomes a daemon-side fix iteration instead of an operator-side cleanup PR. Pre-registration: post-fix, ≥80% of daemon-authored PRs open with zero red CI checks (vs the current ~0% — every PR has needed at least one lint fix).
  - **Details**: (a) New step in the daemon's iteration end-of-task path: before `gh pr create`, run the full CI lint stack as defined by `.github/workflows/ci.yml`'s `needs:` aggregator. (b) If any check fails, the daemon iterates *on the fix* (one more claude --print invocation with the failure message in the brief) instead of opening the PR. (c) Cap retries at 3 per task; if still failing, open the PR with a `Blocked: pre-pr-lint-failures` label so the operator sees it but the gate isn't hidden. (d) Reuses `lefthook`'s `pre-push` hook stack — that's the same gate humans already pass through; the daemon should pass it before opening the PR. (e) Add a self-diagnose invariant `daemon-pr-lint-pass-rate` that watches the rolling 30d pass-rate; if it drops below 80% the invariant fires, suggesting the lint stack drifted ahead of the daemon's brief.
  - **Files**: `novel/tick-loop/src/pre-pr-lint-gate.ts` + tests, `scripts/run-pre-pr-lint-stack.mjs` (the canonical invocation), `novel/tick-loop/bin/tick-loop.mjs` (wire-in), `scripts/self-diagnose.mjs` (new invariant), `docs/daemon-pre-pr-gate.md`.
  - **Touches**: `novel/tick-loop/src/pre-pr-lint-gate.ts`, `novel/tick-loop/src/pre-pr-lint-gate.test.ts`, `scripts/run-pre-pr-lint-stack.mjs`, `novel/tick-loop/bin/tick-loop.mjs`, `scripts/self-diagnose.mjs`, `docs/daemon-pre-pr-gate.md`
  - **Verification**: paired tests for the gate (happy path / one lint fails / 3 retries exhausted / opt-out via Blocked label); integration test that simulates a daemon PR with a deliberate rule-#7 violation and verifies the daemon retries instead of opening.
  - **Measurement**: `pnpm daemon-pr-lint:metrics` prints "Verdict: OK" once the rolling 30d window holds ≥10 PRs (slice 10/N: `scripts/daemon-pr-lint-metrics.mjs` — versioned + paired-tested; the original task-block one-liner used `--author "@minsky-bot"` which never matches a PR in this repo, so the metric was unmeasurable as written). Threshold ≥0.80 pinned in `ROLLING_30D_MIN_PASS_RATE`; minimum sample size ≥10 in `ROLLING_30D_MIN_N`; both mirrored in `scripts/self-diagnose.mjs § daemonPrLintPassRateInvariant`. Also: `node scripts/self-diagnose.mjs --json | jq -e '[.[] | select(.id == "daemon-pr-lint-pass-rate")] | length == 0'` exits 0.
  - **Pivot**: if the lint stack takes >5 min on a daemon iteration (the iteration's claude --print spawn budget is finite), pivot to a *staged* gate — only the fast lints (biome, typecheck, rule-3, rule-12) run pre-PR; the slow ones (vitest, supervisor-integration, lighthouse) gate via CI as today. Pre-PR slow-lints are nice to have but not load-bearing; the fast ones close 80% of the failure modes.
  - **Anchor**: rule #10 (deterministic enforcement — the daemon runs the same gate humans run); rule #2 (the lint stack is already a Strategy seam — `.github/workflows/ci.yml` is the canonical list); rule #9 (the 80% pass-rate is the pre-registered metric); empirical 2026-05-05 (88 iterations, 3 shipped, 5+ operator cleanup PRs).
  - **Risk**: medium. Local lint stack drift vs CI is the main risk — the daemon could pass locally and fail in CI. Mitigation: the `scripts/run-pre-pr-lint-stack.mjs` is canonical; CI imports it. Both run the same script.
  - **Surfaced-by**: 2026-05-05 user request — "I expect minsky to come up with these issues itself" + my analysis "the daemon's first-pass output reliably fails CI lints" identified two daemon self-improvements. This is one.

- [ ] `daemon-fix-own-pr-on-ci-failure` — when daemon's PR fails CI, daemon automatically opens a fix commit on the same branch instead of noop-iterating
  - **ID**: daemon-fix-own-pr-on-ci-failure
  - **Tags**: p0, daemon, supervisor, throughput, self-improvement, pick-next
  - **Milestone**: M1
  - **Pick-next**: yes (operator-flagged 2026-05-05 — supervisor should pick this up immediately after `daemon-pre-pr-lint-gate` ships).
  - **Estimate**: 2d
  - **Hypothesis**: Empirical observation 2026-05-05: with `cross-repo-ci-action` task block in TASKS.md (PR #167 open), the daemon iterated 80+ times producing brief refreshes / noops because the PR can't merge while CI fails, the task can't be removed while the PR is open, and the daemon won't fix CI on its own PR. **Deadlock pattern**. Fix: when the daemon detects its PR exists for the current task AND the PR has failing CI checks, the daemon iteration's brief becomes "fix CI failures on PR #N" instead of "redo the task". Operator never has to intervene; the daemon closes its own loop. Pre-registration: post-fix, time-from-PR-open-to-merge for daemon-authored PRs drops from "indefinite — operator must intervene" to "≤2 daemon iterations median".
  - **Details**: (a) In the daemon's task-pick step (right before claude --print), check if `gh pr list --head <branch>` returns an open PR for the current task ID's branch. (b) If yes AND the PR has failing CI checks, fetch the failure logs (`gh run view --log-failed <id>`) and pass them to claude --print as the brief: "Fix the failing CI checks on PR #N. Failure summary: <log excerpt>". (c) The daemon's claude pushes the fix commit; CI re-runs; next iteration checks again; loop until green or 3 retries. (d) After 3 retries, the daemon labels the PR `Blocked: daemon-stuck` and synthesises a new TASKS.md entry summarising what's blocked (operator-actionable). (e) Composes with `daemon-pre-pr-lint-gate` (above) — that gate cuts most failures at PR-open time; this gate handles the residue.
  - **Files**: `novel/tick-loop/src/daemon-pr-state.ts` + tests (the decision function: open-PR-and-failing? → which-failure-class? → fix-brief), `novel/tick-loop/src/daemon-fix-own-pr.ts` + tests (the fix-iteration entry), `novel/tick-loop/bin/tick-loop.mjs` (wire-in).
  - **Touches**: `novel/tick-loop/src/daemon-pr-state.ts`, `novel/tick-loop/src/daemon-pr-state.test.ts`, `novel/tick-loop/src/daemon-fix-own-pr.ts`, `novel/tick-loop/src/daemon-fix-own-pr.test.ts`, `novel/tick-loop/bin/tick-loop.mjs`
  - **Verification**: paired tests for daemon-pr-state (no-pr / pr-clean / pr-failing / pr-3-retries-exhausted); integration test that opens a synthetic PR with a deliberate failure, runs one daemon iteration, asserts a fix commit landed.
  - **Measurement**: `gh pr list --author "@minsky-bot" --state closed --search "merged:>$(date -v-30d -u +%Y-%m-%d)" --json createdAt,closedAt --jq '[.[] | (((.closedAt|fromdate) - (.createdAt|fromdate)) / 60)] | sort | .[length/2|floor]'` (median minutes from PR open to merge for daemon PRs over rolling 30d) ≤ 240 (4h — accounts for human review on substantive changes; CI-fix-only iterations close in <30min).
  - **Pivot**: if the daemon's fix iteration takes >3 retries reliably (>20% of failing PRs), the failure classes the daemon faces aren't fixable by claude --print alone — pivot to a "human escalation" mode: after 1 retry, the daemon labels the PR `needs-operator` and stops iterating. Don't build infinite-retry — that wastes the budget without producing value.
  - **Anchor**: rule #6 (let-it-crash AT the boundary — the daemon's own PR is its boundary; a green CI is the contract); Beyer SRE 2016 Ch. 6 (the operator-escape-hatch is `Blocked: daemon-stuck`); rule #9 (the 4h median is the pre-registered metric); empirical 2026-05-05 (PR #167's 80-iteration deadlock).
  - **Risk**: medium-high. The daemon could iterate destructively if its fix-brief is wrong (e.g., suppressing a lint instead of fixing the violation). Mitigation: the cap-3 retries + `Blocked: daemon-stuck` escalation; also, the in-tree pre-push lint stack catches obvious destructive changes.
  - **Surfaced-by**: 2026-05-05 user request — "I expect minsky to come up with these issues itself" + my analysis identified the deadlock. This is the second daemon self-improvement.

- [ ] `self-diagnose-iteration-log-probe-format` — fix the `recentIterations` probe in `scripts/self-diagnose.mjs` so it actually parses `.minsky/tick-loop.out.log`
  - **ID**: self-diagnose-iteration-log-probe-format
  - **Tags**: p0, self-diagnose, follow-up
  - **Milestone**: M1
  - **Estimate**: 1d
  - **Hypothesis**: Discovered 2026-05-05 dogfooding PR #184: the `recentIterations` probe expects `JSON.parse(line)` against `{evt: "iteration", taskId, committedSha, ts}` shape, but the supervisor actually emits `[span] tick-loop.iteration {"iteration.index":N,"iteration.status":"completed","task.id":"...","iteration.reason":"..."}` (OTEL span-attribute style, no `committedSha` field). Live `node scripts/self-diagnose.mjs --json` returned `[]` even when the daemon was on iteration 27 of consecutive noops on the same task — `daemon-noop-iteration-rate-too-high` should have fired but couldn't because `readIterationsFromLog` returned `[]`. Pre-registration: after the fix, running self-diagnose against a log with ≥4 consecutive non-committed iterations on one taskId surfaces a `daemon-noop-iteration-rate-too-high` finding. Two parts: (a) align the parser with the live log format (`[span] tick-loop.iteration` prefix + dotted-attribute fields), (b) add a structured `iteration.committed` boolean to the supervisor's iteration span so "noop" detection isn't a heuristic over `iteration.reason` text.
  - **Details**: (a) Update `parseIterationLogLine` in `scripts/self-diagnose.mjs` to strip the `[span] tick-loop.iteration` prefix and read `iteration.index`, `task.id`, `iteration.reason`. (b) Heuristic for `committed`: `iteration.reason.match(/PR #\d+ (opened|merged|landed)/)` OR `git log --since=<iteration.timestamp>` shows a new commit. (c) OR (preferred) extend the supervisor span emit in `novel/tick-loop/src/daemon.ts` to include `iteration.committed: boolean` and `iteration.committedSha: string` directly — then this probe stays simple. (d) Add a regression test that loads a captured-from-prod log fragment and asserts the invariants fire/don't fire as expected.
  - **Files**: `scripts/self-diagnose.mjs` (parser), `scripts/self-diagnose.test.mjs` (regression test against a captured supervisor log fragment), `novel/tick-loop/src/daemon.ts` (optional: structured `iteration.committed` field).
  - **Touches**: `scripts/self-diagnose.mjs`, `scripts/self-diagnose.test.mjs`, `novel/tick-loop/src/daemon.ts`
  - **Verification**: replaying the 2026-05-05 supervisor log through `node scripts/self-diagnose.mjs --json` MUST surface `daemon-noop-iteration-rate-too-high` (the daemon spent iterations 25-27 noop'ing on `daily-changelog-for-humans`).
  - **Measurement**: `node scripts/self-diagnose.mjs --json | jq '[.[] | select(.id == "daemon-noop-iteration-rate-too-high")] | length'` returns ≥1 when invoked against a log with ≥`threshold` consecutive noops; returns 0 otherwise. Live regression: replaying the 2026-05-05 log produces exactly the finding.
  - **Pivot**: if the heuristic for "committed" produces ≥10% false positives over a week, switch to the structured-field approach (option c) — don't retire the invariant.
  - **Acceptance**: (1) parser handles `[span] tick-loop.iteration {...}` lines; (2) regression test loads real supervisor log fragment; (3) `daemon-noop-iteration-rate-too-high` fires on the 2026-05-05 noop streak when replayed.
  - **Anchor**: rule #9 (pre-registered HDD); rule #2 (Strategy seam — production probe vs synthetic test data); Liskov 1987 (invariants must observe the actual system, not a hypothetical one).
  - **Risk**: low. Pure parser fix; substrate already shipped in PR #184.
  - **Surfaced-by**: 2026-05-05 dogfood — the operator ran `node scripts/self-diagnose.mjs --json` against a supervisor stuck in a 4-iteration noop streak; the new throughput invariants returned no findings because the probe couldn't read the live log format.

- [ ] `daemon-priority-discipline-picktask-bug` — `pickTask` must consult `**Tags**:`, not just file ordering, so a misplaced p1-tagged block in the `## P0` section can no longer shadow genuine P0 work
  - **ID**: daemon-priority-discipline-picktask-bug
  - **Tags**: p0, daemon, supervisor, throughput, priority-discipline, follow-up
  - **Milestone**: M1
  - **Estimate**: 1d
  - **Hypothesis**: Discovered 2026-05-05 dogfooding: the daemon picked `daily-changelog-for-humans` (P1) for 30 iterations and `canonical-metric-list-per-repo` (P1) for 6 iterations while genuine P0 work — `daemon-pre-pr-lint-gate`, `daemon-fix-own-pr-on-ci-failure`, `daemon-self-detect-throughput-issues`, 6 security P0s — sat unclaimed all session. Root cause: `pickTask` (in `novel/tick-loop/src/daemon.ts`) walks file ordering inside the `## P0` + `## P1` slice and returns the first unclaimed/unblocked task. A p1-tagged block physically positioned in the `## P0` section therefore beats every P0 below it. Pre-registration: after the fix, replaying today's TASKS.md state produces a different `pickTask` result (the first p0-tagged block — `post-task-cto-audit`) instead of the first physical-section block. The textual fix in this PR is the brief tightening — `extractOpenP0TaskIds` + the priority-discipline gate makes the misordering visible-not-silent. The architectural fix is `pickTask` itself reading `**Tags**:` and refusing non-p0 entries when scanning `## P0`. The brief gate is belt-and-suspenders; the picker fix is the real correction.
  - **Details**: (a) Extend `pickTask` in `novel/tick-loop/src/daemon.ts` so that within the `## P0` slice, only blocks whose `**Tags**:` line includes `p0` are eligible. (b) Same logic for `## P1`: only `p1`-tagged blocks. (c) Reuse the existing `extractOpenP0TaskIds` helper (this PR). (d) Replay test against today's TASKS.md state — the picked task must change from `canonical-metric-list-per-repo` to `post-task-cto-audit` (or whichever genuine p0 is first). (e) Optional: lint that fails CI when a block in `## P0` is tagged `p1` or vice-versa — keeps TASKS.md hygienic without manual review.
  - **Files**: `novel/tick-loop/src/daemon.ts` (extend `pickTask`), `novel/tick-loop/src/daemon.test.ts` (paired tests + replay test against today's TASKS.md state), optionally `scripts/check-tasks-md-tag-section-alignment.mjs`.
  - **Touches**: `novel/tick-loop/src/daemon.ts`, `novel/tick-loop/src/daemon.test.ts`, `scripts/check-tasks-md-tag-section-alignment.mjs`
  - **Verification**: replay today's TASKS.md against `pickTask` BEFORE the fix returns the misordered task; AFTER the fix returns the highest-priority `p0`-tagged unclaimed block.
  - **Measurement**: `node -e 'import("./novel/tick-loop/dist/index.js").then(m => console.log(m.pickTask(require("fs").readFileSync("TASKS.md", "utf8"))))'` — before fix returns the misordered block; after fix returns the highest-priority p0.
  - **Pivot**: if the strict `Tags`-aware picker false-skips because of inconsistent tag formatting (e.g., `P0` uppercase vs `p0`), normalise case-insensitively rather than retire the discipline. If the lint catches too many legitimate operator overrides, allow `## P0` entries tagged `p1, pick-next-p0-override` as an explicit promotion path.
  - **Acceptance**: (1) `pickTask` consults `**Tags**:` and returns only tag-aligned blocks within each section; (2) regression test loads today's TASKS.md state and asserts the picked task changes; (3) the brief's priority-discipline gate becomes redundant once this lands (the gate stays as belt-and-suspenders).
  - **Anchor**: rule #2 (Strategy seam — picker is pure, fed by tasksMd content); rule #9 (pre-registered HDD); rule #10 (deterministic enforcement — tag/section alignment is a CI lint, not a hope); operator 2026-05-05 — "does it follow /next-task queue as expected?".
  - **Risk**: low. Pure picker change; covered by paired tests; the brief gate (this PR) keeps the daemon honest in the meantime.
  - **Surfaced-by**: 2026-05-05 operator question — "Is it working efficiently, eg does it follow /next-task queue as expected?". Investigation revealed daemon picked P1 tasks 36/53 iterations (68%) while P0 work sat unclaimed.

- [ ] `daemon-pr-self-grade-format-baked` — bake the strict pr-self-grade format into `buildDaemonBrief` so daemon never ships malformed `**Match:**` blocks
  - **ID**: daemon-pr-self-grade-format-baked
  - **Tags**: p1, daemon, supervisor, throughput, brief, follow-up
  - **Milestone**: M1
  - **Touches**: `novel/tick-loop/src/daemon.ts`, `novel/tick-loop/src/daemon.test.ts`, `scripts/pre-push-pr-self-grade.mjs`
  - **Estimate**: 0.5d
  - **Hypothesis**: Discovered via 2026-05-05 speed-comparison measurement (operator-vs-daemon PR open→merge): daemon ships PR bodies with `**Predicted.**` / `**Match:** Yes` (period or colon-inside-bold), but the `pr-self-grade` lint regex requires `**Predicted**:` / `**Match**: yes` (colon-outside-bold, lowercase value). Daemon hits this on ~10% of PRs, costing 1-2 fix iterations each (~30min wasted per occurrence; observed concretely on PR #201 today, two iterations). Pre-registration: with the format baked into the brief as a copy-paste-ready template, daemon's pr-self-grade fail rate drops below 2%/week (down from observed ~10%); median PR open→merge for daemon drops by ≥3 minutes (the median fix-and-rebase round-trip for this specific failure).
  - **Details**: (a) Extend `buildDaemonBrief` with a `## PR self-grade template (copy-paste verbatim)` section emitting the exact strict format: `- **Predicted**: …` / `- **Observed**: …` / `- **Match**: yes | no | partial` / `- **Lesson**: …`. (b) Add an explicit "DO NOT MODIFY THIS FORMAT" anti-rewrite directive — claude tends to reformat to its own bold/period preference. (c) Add a paired test that asserts the brief contains the exact substrings the lint regex matches. (d) Optional: add a pre-push hook that runs `node scripts/check-pr-self-grade.mjs <pr-body-file>` before opening the PR (bake-out the failure rather than catching it post-spawn).
  - **Files**: `novel/tick-loop/src/daemon.ts` (extend `buildDaemonBrief`), `novel/tick-loop/src/daemon.test.ts` (paired tests for the new section), optionally `scripts/pre-push-pr-self-grade.mjs`.
  - **Verification**: spawn daemon iteration with the new brief; assert the produced PR body matches `scripts/check-pr-self-grade.mjs` regex on first try (no fix iteration). Replay PR #201's body shape: with old brief → fails; with new brief → passes.
  - **Measurement**: rolling 7d post-merge: `gh pr list --author '@me' --state all --search 'created:>=YYYY-MM-DD' --json statusCheckRollup --jq '[.[] | select(.statusCheckRollup[] | select(.name=="pr-self-grade" and .conclusion=="FAILURE"))] | length / [.] | length'` returns < 0.02 (was ~0.10 pre-fix).
  - **Pivot**: if daemon still mis-formats despite the baked template, switch from "copy-paste this template" to "the harness writes the self-grade block FROM YOUR ITERATION OUTPUT" — i.e., move format generation out of claude's hands entirely. Don't retire the lint.
  - **Acceptance**: (1) brief carries copy-paste-ready PR self-grade template; (2) anti-rewrite directive present; (3) paired test asserts substring match; (4) post-merge 7d rolling pr-self-grade fail rate < 2%.
  - **Anchor**: 2026-05-05 operator-vs-daemon speed measurement (median open→merge: operator ~4 min, daemon ~23 min; ~6× gap); rule #2 (Strategy seam — pure brief builder); rule #9 (pre-registered HDD); rule #10 (deterministic enforcement — the lint regex IS the format spec); composes with `daemon-self-optimize-speed-tokens`.
  - **Risk**: low. Pure-builder extension; covered by paired tests; the lint regex is already deterministic.
  - **Surfaced-by**: 2026-05-05 operator directive — "Measure speed of implementing a minsky feature here in claude code vs implementing it through minsky itself. Open tasks to improve it." Speed-comparison surfaced PR-self-grade-format-loop as a recurring 30min-cost failure mode.

- [ ] `self-diagnose-on-start` — supervisor probes invariants at boot, escalates failures via TASKS.md
  - **ID**: self-diagnose-on-start
  - **Tags**: observability, supervisor, self-detection, rule-7, rule-9
  - **Milestone**: M1
  - **Touches**: `scripts/self-diagnose*.mjs`, `distribution/systemd/run-tick-loop.sh`, `novel/tick-loop/src/daemon.ts`, `docs/self-diagnose-authoring.md`
  - **Estimate**: 3d
  - **Hypothesis**: every dogfood-surfaced bug to date has been "running state silently violates an invariant the operator-only-noticed-after-the-fact": (a) cache_read sum bug pegged every plan to 100 % used and only the operator noticed the homogeneous `budget-paused` log stream; (b) dashboard's 10 metric values stayed `(stub)` indefinitely because no probe asserted "after T minutes uptime, ≥1 metric should resolve to a real value". Encoding these invariants in `scripts/self-diagnose.mjs` and running them at supervisor start (and on a 5-min tick thereafter) means the next equivalent bug surfaces as a P0 TASKS.md entry within 5 min of regression — escalated automatically through the channel `/next-task` already drains. Pre-registered metric: time-from-regression-to-task-block-existing drops from "human notices, multiple hours" to "≤5 min" for the class of invariant-violation bugs. v0 ships with the seed invariant (`token-monitor-not-all-pegged`), the runner, the writer, and the supervisor wire-in. Subsequent invariants are appended in follow-up PRs (each one a 1-file change).
  - **Details**: (a) `scripts/self-diagnose.mjs` (already shipped in this PR) — pure invariant runner + seed invariant + TASKS.md writer + CLI. (b) `distribution/systemd/run-tick-loop.sh` runs `node scripts/self-diagnose.mjs --write-tasks-md --append-to TASKS.md` once at supervisor start; failures are written into the P0 section but the supervisor continues (advisory, not blocking — rule #7 graceful-degrade). (c) Add a 5-min `setInterval` probe in `tick-loop` to re-run invariants between iterations; on transition from `ok→violation` for any invariant, emit an OTEL span `tick-loop.self-diagnose.violation-detected` and a notifier push (the same surface budget-paused uses). (d) Each new invariant is a 1-export addition to `scripts/self-diagnose.mjs` + paired test; the runner accepts them automatically. (e) Document the invariant-authoring pattern in `docs/self-diagnose-authoring.md` — "what makes an invariant: must hold under every legal supervisor state; must fail loudly under at least one observed bug; must carry suggestedTaskTitle + suggestedFix that themselves comply with rule #9."
  - **Files**: `scripts/self-diagnose.mjs` (this PR), `scripts/self-diagnose.test.mjs` (this PR), `distribution/systemd/run-tick-loop.sh` (this PR — supervisor wire-in), `novel/tick-loop/src/daemon.ts` (follow-up — interval probe), `docs/self-diagnose-authoring.md` (follow-up).
  - **Verification**: (a) `pnpm vitest run scripts/self-diagnose.test.mjs` — 7 paired tests covering runner + seed invariant + writer (already passing). (b) Supervisor log on next start contains `self-diagnose:` line. (c) When the cache_read regression is re-introduced (revert PR #155 in a scratch branch), `node scripts/self-diagnose.mjs` exits 1 and surfaces the invariant.
  - **Measurement**: `node scripts/self-diagnose.mjs --json | jq '. | length'` → integer count of violations; on a clean supervisor it is `0`. Regression test: `git stash; git revert <PR#155-merge-sha> --no-commit; node scripts/self-diagnose.mjs --json | jq -e '[.[] | select(.id == "token-monitor-not-all-pegged")] | length == 1'` exits 0 (the seed invariant catches the seeded bug). Tracking metric: time-to-task-block-existing for invariant-class regressions, measured manually at first (one data point per regression), promoted to OTEL span `self-diagnose.violation-detected.duration` once ≥3 invariants ship.
  - **Pivot**: if invariants false-positive ≥1 task per week (probe drift or transient supervisor states getting flagged), add a `consecutiveFailures: 2` retry gate before surfacing — the runner becomes "fail twice in a row, then escalate" rather than "fail once, escalate". Don't retire the architecture; tune the probe-stability axis. If even with retries the false-positive rate stays high (≥1/week sustained 4 weeks), pivot from "invariant set + boot probe" to "anomaly detection over OpenObserve metrics" — but that requires the span→metric pipeline to be wired (separate P0; out of scope here).
  - **Acceptance**: (1) seed invariant ships and catches the cache_read bug under revert; (2) supervisor logs show self-diagnose ran at boot; (3) writing findings to TASKS.md round-trips through `/next-task` (a self-detected task is pickable like any other); (4) ≥6 paired tests cover the runner + seed invariant + writer.
  - **Anchor**: Liskov 1987 (invariants as the substrate of correctness — invariant violation IS the bug); Brilliant, Knight, Leveson, "Analysis of Faults in an N-Version Software Experiment", *IEEE TSE* 1990 (one version probes the other); Hewitt 1973 (TASKS.md as actor message store — escalation channel reuse); rule #7 (graceful degrade — supervisor never blocks on self-diagnose); rule #9 (every violation carries hypothesis + measurement + pivot + anchor in its synthesised task block).
  - **Risk**: medium. The probe surface is the runtime state of every novel package; an invariant that's wrong is itself a bug source. Mitigation: each invariant ships with paired tests pinning its decision function, and the Pivot threshold (≥1 false-positive/week) is the explicit retire signal.
  - **Surfaced-by**: 2026-05-04 user brief — "i want minsky to be able to detect bugs in its own launched state. so maybe as soon as minsky starts, it also analyzes its own bugs and delegates fixing itself to the parent". Seed invariant (`token-monitor-not-all-pegged`) directly encodes the bug PR #155 fixed: had this invariant been live before that PR, the cache_read regression would have surfaced as a self-detected P0 the moment the supervisor restarted.

- [ ] `cross-repo-runner-budget-aware-halt` — distinguish claude budget exhaustion from systemic `spawn-failed` in `--loop` / `--hosts-dir` mode and retry-with-backoff so fleet loops survive budget pauses without operator restart
  - **ID**: cross-repo-runner-budget-aware-halt
  - **Tags**: p0, cross-repo-runner, resilience, automation, operator-babysitting
  - **Milestone**: M1
  - **Estimate**: 0.5d
  - **Hypothesis**: `novel/cross-repo-runner/bin/minsky-run.mjs` (line 28) imports `ProcessSpawnStrategy` directly — no `LlmProviderSpawnStrategy` wrapper. When claude's budget is exhausted mid-fleet-loop, `runLive` returns `verdict: spawn-failed` and `host-loop.ts` line 283 (`if (outcome.verdict === "spawn-failed") return "spawn-failed"`) immediately halts the loop. The CLI exits non-zero. In `--loop` and `--hosts-dir` mode the operator must restart manually — the same babysitting pattern the tick-loop eliminated via `LlmProviderSpawnStrategy`. Meanwhile the tick-loop daemon wires `LlmProviderSpawnStrategy` which calls `isClaudeHardLimit(stderrTail)` to distinguish budget-exhausted from systemic failure. `isClaudeHardLimit` is already exported from `@minsky/tick-loop` (index.ts:533). Pre-registration: post-fix, when `runLive` returns `spawn-failed` AND `isClaudeHardLimit(stderrTail)` is true, the `host-loop` enters a configurable hold: sleep `MINSKY_CROSS_REPO_BUDGET_RETRY_WAIT_MS` (default 300000 ms — 5 min, the cache-refresh window) then re-pick and retry, up to `MINSKY_CROSS_REPO_BUDGET_MAX_RETRIES` times (default 12 — 1 h total hold). After max retries, surface `"claude-budget-exhausted"` stop reason and exit with code 5 (operator restarts when budget is back). `grep -c "spawn-failed" .minsky/cross-repo-runner.log` over rolling 7d during a budget-exhaustion event drops from ≥1 (fleet halts immediately) to 0 (fleet holds; `"claude-budget-exhausted"` fires only after max-retries).
  - **Details**: (a) New `"claude-budget-exhausted"` variant in `LoopStopReason` (`host-loop.ts`) and `WalkerStopReason` (`host-walker.ts`). (b) In `runHostLoop`: after `verdict === "spawn-failed"`, call `isClaudeHardLimit({ stderrTail: outcome.stderrTail })` — if true, increment `budgetRetryCount`; if `budgetRetryCount < maxRetries`, `await sleep(retryWaitMs)` and continue the loop. If `budgetRetryCount >= maxRetries`, return `{ stopReason: "claude-budget-exhausted" }`. If not a hard-limit, return `"spawn-failed"` immediately (systemic failure, no retry). (c) `host-walker.ts`: propagate `"claude-budget-exhausted"` upward; only `"spawn-failed"` (systemic) halts the whole walker immediately. (d) CLI exit codes: `spawn-failed` → exit 1 (unchanged), `claude-budget-exhausted` → exit 5 (new — signals "wait and retry"). (e) Env vars: `MINSKY_CROSS_REPO_BUDGET_RETRY_WAIT_MS` (default 300000) and `MINSKY_CROSS_REPO_BUDGET_MAX_RETRIES` (default 12) plumbed from CLI env into `runHostLoop`'s opts. (f) Three new paired tests in `host-loop.test.ts`: hard-limit-then-succeed (retry path works), hard-limit-max-retries-exhausted (`"claude-budget-exhausted"` stop reason fires), non-hard-limit-spawn-failed (immediate `"spawn-failed"`, no retry).
  - **Files**: `novel/cross-repo-runner/src/host-loop.ts`, `novel/cross-repo-runner/src/host-loop.test.ts`, `novel/cross-repo-runner/src/host-walker.ts`, `novel/cross-repo-runner/src/host-walker.test.ts`, `novel/cross-repo-runner/bin/minsky-run.mjs`
  - **Touches**: `novel/cross-repo-runner/src/host-loop.ts`, `novel/cross-repo-runner/src/host-loop.test.ts`, `novel/cross-repo-runner/src/host-walker.ts`, `novel/cross-repo-runner/src/host-walker.test.ts`, `novel/cross-repo-runner/bin/minsky-run.mjs`
  - **Verification**: (1) `host-loop.test.ts` — hard-limit on iter 1, succeed on iter 2 → `"validated"` stop; (2) hard-limit across all retries → `"claude-budget-exhausted"` stop; (3) non-hard-limit `spawn-failed` → immediate halt, retry count stays 0. Integration: `MINSKY_CROSS_REPO_BUDGET_MAX_RETRIES=1 MINSKY_CROSS_REPO_BUDGET_RETRY_WAIT_MS=100 minsky-run --host <dir> --loop` with a shim that returns hard-limit stderr on first call, succeeds on second → asserts exit 0, loop ran 2 iterations.
  - **Measurement**: `grep -c "spawn-failed" .minsky/cross-repo-runner.log` during a budget-exhaustion event = 0 (was ≥1). `grep -c "claude-budget-exhausted" .minsky/cross-repo-runner.log` ≥ 1 per exhaustion event (new observable signal). Pivot threshold: if retry logic produces false holds — `isClaudeHardLimit` misclassifies a transient rate-limit as hard-limit, triggering a 1 h pause — tighten the classifier or lower `MINSKY_CROSS_REPO_BUDGET_MAX_RETRIES` default to 3 (15 min) before retiring.
  - **Pivot**: if `isClaudeHardLimit` false-positive rate ≥10%/week on transient rate-limit errors, default `MINSKY_CROSS_REPO_BUDGET_MAX_RETRIES=0` (classify and surface `"claude-budget-exhausted"` immediately with exit 5, no hold) — the operator gets the diagnostic exit code but the retry loop is opt-in. Don't retire the stop-reason distinction; that is load-bearing for operator-visible diagnosis regardless of the retry policy.
  - **Acceptance**: (1) `LoopStopReason` and `WalkerStopReason` include `"claude-budget-exhausted"`; (2) 3 new paired tests; (3) exit code 5 for `"claude-budget-exhausted"`; (4) env vars plumbed; (5) live fleet loop survives a budget exhaustion for up to `maxRetries × retryWaitMs` without operator intervention.
  - **Anchor**: `novel/cross-repo-runner/bin/minsky-run.mjs` line 28 (bare `ProcessSpawnStrategy`, no `LlmProviderSpawnStrategy`); `novel/cross-repo-runner/src/host-loop.ts` line 283 (`spawn-failed → immediate return`, no hard-limit discrimination); `isClaudeHardLimit` already exported from `@minsky/tick-loop/src/llm-provider-selector.ts` (index.ts:533); PRs #489–#492 (cross-repo-runner fleet live, 2026-05-12) — runner operational but inherits none of the budget-resilience from the tick-loop's months of work. Rule #6 (let-it-crash AT the right boundary — budget exhaustion is the OPERATOR's boundary, not the runner's; the runner holds until the operator's budget recovers). Rule #9 (pre-registered HDD — measurement command above). Beyer et al., *SRE*, 2016, Ch. 6 (make the system noisily fail the moment it detects a defect — today `spawn-failed` swallows the budget signal silently).
  - **Risk**: low. The change is additive — existing `spawn-failed` behavior is preserved when `isClaudeHardLimit` returns false. The max-retries ceiling ensures the retry loop can't spin indefinitely.
  - **Surfaced-by**: CTO audit 2026-05-12 post `minsky-runtime-resilience` ship — code review of `novel/cross-repo-runner/bin/minsky-run.mjs` vs. `novel/tick-loop/bin/tick-loop.mjs`; the fleet-scale runner (PRs #489–#492, 2026-05-12) inherits none of the budget-resilience work that took months to land for the local daemon.

- [ ] `cto-audit-skip-tasksmd-only-iterations` — guard `shouldRunCtoAudit` against TASKS.md-only commits (no PR opened) so the audit does not fire on progress-update-only iterations
  - **ID**: cto-audit-skip-tasksmd-only-iterations
  - **Tags**: p0, automation, cto-audit, noop-guard
  - **Milestone**: M1
  - **Estimate**: 0.25d
  - **Hypothesis**: `shouldRunCtoAudit` (`novel/tick-loop/src/post-task-cto-audit.ts:163`) fires when `filesChanged.length > 0 || prUrl !== null`. A TASKS.md-only commit (`filesChanged = ["TASKS.md"]`, `prUrl = null`) satisfies this gate, triggering a full CTO audit spawn. The audit's four leverage criteria — automation gaps, instrumentation gaps, duplicated patterns, recurring failure classes — cannot be satisfied when no code artifact shipped: the just-completed iteration is a progress-update write, not a feature ship. Each wasted audit: opens a branch (`audit/<date>-<task-id>`), runs CI on that branch, creates a PR the daemon must track, and advances the CTO audit PR count metric without any new task filed. Concrete evidence: the audit triggering this block was fired by `minsky-cross-machine-dotfile-checks`, which produced only `TASKS.md` changes (no PR). Pre-registration: post-fix, when all entries in `filesChanged` are `TASKS.md` (or `tasks/<id>.md` once the per-task migration ships) AND `prUrl === null`, `shouldRunCtoAudit` returns `false` with a new `"tasks-md-only-noop"` skip reason. The `tick-loop.cto-audit.skip_reason` span attribute captures the reason; the dashboard audit-firing-rate tile shows the saved cycles. Test count: +2 paired tests (TASKS.md-only noop → false; TASKS.md + one code file → true).
  - **Details**: (a) In `shouldRunCtoAudit`, after the existing `filesChanged.length === 0 && prUrl === null` early-exit, add: `if (args.filesChanged.every(f => f === "TASKS.md" || /^tasks\/[^/]+\.md$/.test(f)) && args.prUrl === null) return false;` — one line. (b) Extend `SkipReason` union (`post-task-cto-audit.ts:208`) with `"tasks-md-only-noop"`. (c) Update `runCtoAudit`'s internal call to `shouldRunCtoAudit` to surface the new skip reason in the returned `RunCtoAuditOutcome`. (d) Paired tests in `post-task-cto-audit.test.ts`: TASKS.md-only + no PR → `false`; single code file + TASKS.md + no PR → `true`; TASKS.md-only + PR URL present → `true` (a TASKS.md-only commit that DOES open a PR is a legitimate audit surface — e.g., a pure TASKS.md refactor that ships as a PR).
  - **Files**: `novel/tick-loop/src/post-task-cto-audit.ts` (guard + new skip reason), `novel/tick-loop/src/post-task-cto-audit.test.ts` (2 new paired tests).
  - **Touches**: `novel/tick-loop/src/post-task-cto-audit.ts`, `novel/tick-loop/src/post-task-cto-audit.test.ts`
  - **Verification**: paired tests cover the 3 gate cases above. Live regression: run the daemon against a synthetic iteration whose only change is `TASKS.md` (no PR); assert `tick-loop.cto-audit.skip_reason = "tasks-md-only-noop"` appears in the span log and no audit branch is opened.
  - **Measurement**: `grep -c '"tasks-md-only-noop"' .minsky/tick-loop.out.log` over rolling 7d ≥ 1 (proves the guard fires on real noop iterations — baseline from `minsky-cross-machine-dotfile-checks` today). AND `gh pr list --label minsky:cto-audit --state all --json body --jq '[.[] | select(.body | contains("Files changed (1):\n  - TASKS.md") and contains("(no PR opened)"))] | length'` over rolling 7d = 0 (was 1 before this fix — exhibit: the PR that contains this task block).
  - **Pivot**: if the guard is too aggressive (a TASKS.md-only commit that is itself a major task-queue restructuring warrants a CTO audit), add an opt-in escape hatch: `MINSKY_CTO_AUDIT_FORCE=1` in the iteration's env skips the guard for that iteration only. Don't widen the guard to "all TASKS.md-touching iterations" — a commit that includes code + TASKS.md is a real ship.
  - **Acceptance**: (1) `shouldRunCtoAudit({ filesChanged: ["TASKS.md"], prUrl: null, ... })` returns `false`; (2) `SkipReason` includes `"tasks-md-only-noop"`; (3) 2 new paired tests pass; (4) no audit branch opens on the next TASKS.md-only daemon iteration.
  - **Anchor**: `novel/tick-loop/src/post-task-cto-audit.ts:163` (the gate that currently passes TASKS.md-only commits); `minsky-cross-machine-dotfile-checks` iteration 2026-05-12 (exhibit: TASKS.md-only output triggered this audit, which produced this task block — a self-referential proof that the guard is needed); rule #9 (pre-registered HDD — measurement command above); rule #10 (deterministic enforcement — the fix is a one-line predicate, not a hint); Ries 2011 (vanity metric guard — the CTO audit PR count must measure leverage, not iteration volume).
  - **Risk**: low. One-line predicate addition behind paired tests. The guard is conservative — it only fires when ALL changed files are TASKS.md AND no PR was opened. Any iteration that ships even one line of code bypasses the guard.
  - **Surfaced-by**: CTO audit 2026-05-12 — the `minsky-cross-machine-dotfile-checks` iteration produced only `TASKS.md` changes (no PR), yet `shouldRunCtoAudit` returned `true` and the full audit spawn ran. The audit's brief confirmed "Files changed (1): TASKS.md; PR: (no PR opened)" — zero code artifact to analyze. Self-referential: this task block is the audit's output from a wasted audit cycle.
- [ ] `devin-spawn-missing-permission-mode-bypass` — minsky-run.mjs spawns Devin without `--permission-mode bypass` so every Devin worker is blocked from `edit`/`write`/`exec` on the very first call ("Running in non-interactive mode. Use --permission-mode dangerous to auto-approve all tools."). Devin workers cannot ship ANY task while this is the case, including walker-drains-one-host-forever.
  - **ID**: devin-spawn-missing-permission-mode-bypass
  - **Tags**: p0, milestone-m1, reliability, cross-repo-runner, devin, blocker, prereq
  - **Details**: Root cause confirmed live 2026-05-18 from a spawned Devin worker assigned walker-drains-one-host-forever — every `edit`/`write`/`exec`/`ask_user_question`/`request_scope` call returned `Tool execution was rejected: Running in non-interactive mode. Use --permission-mode dangerous to auto-approve all tools.` Only `read`, `grep`, `glob`, and pre-approved MCP tools (`mcp__tasks-mcp__*`, `mcp__playwright__*`, …) succeeded. Source of the bug: `novel/cross-repo-runner/bin/minsky-run.mjs` `buildAgentConfig` Devin branch (lines 938–957) constructs `argv = ["--print", "--model", model, "--prompt-file", promptPath]`. There is NO `--permission-mode` flag. Per Devin CLI docs (`~/.local/share/devin/cli/_versions/2026.5.6-8/share/devin/docs/reference/permissions.mdx` line 19–22, 26): Normal mode (the default) auto-approves only read-only ops; bash/edit/write require explicit human approval which is impossible under `--print` with no TTY. The Claude Code branch right below it already passes `--permission-mode bypassPermissions` (line 962–965) and works for the same reason this fails. While this remains unfixed, the entire Devin-agent surface of the fleet is a zero-throughput no-op — the daemon logs will show `spawn-failed` (or worse, `validated` with no actual edits) on every iteration. FIX (one line): in `buildAgentConfig` for `cmd === "devin"`, before `--prompt-file`, push `"--permission-mode", "bypass"` (or `"dangerous"` — same thing per devin docs alias rules). Optional follow-up: plumb a `MINSKY_DEVIN_PERMISSION_MODE` env override identical to the existing `MINSKY_CLAUDE_PERMISSION_MODE` so the operator can dial down if needed.
  - **Files**: novel/cross-repo-runner/bin/minsky-run.mjs
  - **Acceptance**: (1) After fix, `minsky-run --host <host> --live` against a Devin agent successfully runs `edit`/`write`/`exec` tools without permission rejection. (2) Test: spawn a 1-iter loop against a fixture host whose only task asks the worker to `echo hi > /tmp/devin-perm-test`; verify the file gets written. (3) `grep 'Running in non-interactive mode' ~/.minsky/daemon.log` drops from ≥1 to 0 across a 10-iteration walk.

<!-- CTO audit 2026-05-19 after walker-drains-one-host-forever (#644): the walker now fairly distributes iterations across hosts, but the aggregate iteration→PR ship rate is the next unmeasured surface. Filed below. -->

- [ ] `cloud-agent-spawn-argv-regression-matrix` — every cloud agent (claude, devin, aider, future-N) declares its required argv contract in a single fixture file; a parameterized test matrix asserts that spawning each agent without any required flag yields a recognizable failure mode, AND that spawning each agent WITH the contract argv succeeds — same `it.each(MATRIX)` shape that agentbrew PR #1021 used for its (agent × format × env-var) crash-class regression
  - **ID**: cloud-agent-spawn-argv-regression-matrix
  - **Tags**: p0, milestone-m1, rule-3a, rule-3b, rule-9, rule-17, test, spawn, regression-suite, agentbrew-parallel
  - **Milestone**: M1
  - **Surfaced-by**: 2026-05-21 cross-repo session. The agentbrew session shipped a (15-agent × per-feature × per-env-var) regression matrix that catches the equivalent Devin-import-crash class in 92 + 79 + 43 fixture-driven tests across PRs #1021 / #1022 / #1023 plus a slow-tier real-Devin CI workflow. Minsky has the same crash-class shape — `spawn-failed exit=-1 stderr empty` is the live P0 above — but no parameterized regression matrix. Today, fixing devin's spawn-failed verdict relies on operator memory of "did I propagate `--permission-mode dangerous`?"; tomorrow, adding a 4th cloud agent means manually replicating that memory. This task encodes the contract per-agent in a fixture so the regression class stays shut as the spawn surface grows.
  - **Hypothesis**: building a single `novel/cross-repo-runner/test/fixtures/cloud-agent-matrix.ts` whose rows are (agentName, requiredArgv[], briefDeliveryMode, expectedFailurePatternsWhenFlagMissing[], expectedSuccessPatternsWhenAllFlagsPresent[]), then running `it.each(MATRIX)` over the matrix from a fixed fast-tier vitest file, will reduce per-agent argv-regression bug class to a single-line fixture edit when a new agent ships AND will fail loudly within the same CI run as any regression — measured by mean-time-to-detect dropping from "next 10h daemon run with stability < 50%" to "<5 minutes in CI".
  - **Success**: (1) 3-agent fast-tier matrix (claude, devin, aider) runs in <2s and 9 properties × 3 agents = 27+ tests pass on green; (2) deliberately stripping `--permission-mode dangerous` from devin's MATRIX row causes ≥1 fast-tier test to fail with a message that names the missing flag; (3) the slow-tier workflow at `.github/workflows/cloud-agent-spawn-real-tier.yml` invokes the real binary for whichever agents are installed on the runner and exits 77 when none are, never silently green; (4) the spawn-class verdict rate (verdict ∈ {spawn-failed, exit=-1}) on the `host-daemon loop` 7-day rolling window drops from the 2026-05-19 baseline (~90% of all spawns) to ≤10% within 2 weeks of merge.
  - **Pivot**: if 3 fix attempts inside this task fail to keep both fast-tier and slow-tier passing for ≥48h consecutively (i.e., the matrix itself is too flaky to live), retire the fixture-driven approach for minsky and ship a per-agent hand-rolled spawn smoke test instead (one `bin-minsky-<agent>-smoke.test.ts` file per agent, no shared fixture). Threshold: ≥3 flake-incidents on the fast-tier in the first 48h of CI runs ⇒ pivot.
  - **Measurement**: `pnpm vitest run novel/cross-repo-runner/test/cloud-agent-spawn-argv-matrix.test.ts --reporter=json | jq '.testResults[0].testResults | map({status,fullName}) | group_by(.status) | map({status: .[0].status, count: length})'` produces `[{"status":"passed","count":27+}]` on green; the same command after manually deleting a required flag from `cloud-agent-matrix.ts` produces ≥1 row with `"status":"failed"`. AND `tail -300 ~/.minsky/daemon.log | awk '/verdict=spawn-failed/{f++} END{print f}'` falls from today's baseline (~30 per 7d) to <3 per 7d in the 2 weeks post-merge (cross-checked against `pnpm stability-number`).
  - **Anchor**: Wynne & Hellesøy *The Cucumber Book* (Pragmatic Bookshelf, 2012) Ch. 1 — parameterized acceptance scenarios over an example table is the canonical pattern for "same behavior, many shapes"; this task instantiates it as `it.each(MATRIX)` over the cloud-agent surface. Havelund & Goldberg *Runtime Verification 2008* — the substrate already shipped in `novel/cross-repo-runner/src/runtime-invariants.ts` validates the spawn contract per-iteration; this task adds the *test-time* counterpart so the same invariants fail CI not just live runs. Composes with rule #3a (runtime invariants — coverage ≠ correctness, which explicitly cites devin-spawn-failures as the failure class unit tests miss); rule #3b (integration tests for CLI features). Direct sibling: [agentbrew PR #1021](https://github.example.com/example-org/agentbrew/pull/1021) — same `it.each(AGENT_MATRIX)` pattern locking down the equivalent crash class on the agentbrew config-sync surface, where 9 fixtures × 4 properties = 36 fast-tier tests plus a real-Devin slow tier turned a recurring class of crashes into a single-row fixture edit.
  - **Details**: (a) Create `novel/cross-repo-runner/test/fixtures/cloud-agent-matrix.ts` exporting `CLOUD_AGENT_MATRIX: CloudAgentFixture[]` with one row per supported agent. Schema per row: `{ name: "claude" | "devin" | "aider", requiredArgv: string[], briefDelivery: "stdin" | "prompt-file" | "message-file", modelFlag: "--model", successPatterns: RegExp[], failurePatterns: { whenMissing: { flag: string, pattern: RegExp }[] } }`. (b) Create `novel/cross-repo-runner/test/cloud-agent-spawn-argv-matrix.test.ts` with parameterized tests: "row.name produces successPattern when spawned with full argv", "row.name fails with row.failurePatterns.whenMissing[N].pattern when row.requiredArgv minus that flag is spawned", "row.name's briefDelivery mode matches what `novel/cross-repo-runner/src/agent-launcher.ts` actually passes". (c) Add a simulator at `novel/cross-repo-runner/src/cloud-agent-simulator.ts` — pure function `simulateAgentSpawn(name, argv, briefBytes): { ok, stdout, stderr, signal }` that documents each agent's strict-arg contract in code (no real spawn — same pattern as agentbrew's `devin-import.simulator.ts` for the strict-interpolation case). (d) Add slow-tier workflow `.github/workflows/cloud-agent-spawn-real-tier.yml` that on nightly schedule + `mcp-devin`/`real-spawn` PR label spawns each installed agent with a noop brief, asserts exit 0 / non-empty stdout, exits 77 if the agent isn't on the runner. (e) Add `pnpm test:cloud-agent-real-tier` script that runs the same logic locally.
  - **Files**: `novel/cross-repo-runner/test/fixtures/cloud-agent-matrix.ts` (new — fixture matrix); `novel/cross-repo-runner/test/cloud-agent-spawn-argv-matrix.test.ts` (new — parameterized regression suite); `novel/cross-repo-runner/src/cloud-agent-simulator.ts` (new — strict-arg simulator); `novel/cross-repo-runner/test/cloud-agent-simulator.test.ts` (new — anchors the simulator's contract); `.github/workflows/cloud-agent-spawn-real-tier.yml` (new — slow-tier nightly); `scripts/cloud-agent-real-tier.mjs` (new — local runner, exits 77 on missing agent); `package.json` (add `test:cloud-agent-real-tier` script).
  - **Acceptance**: GIVEN the fast-tier matrix file exists, WHEN `pnpm vitest run novel/cross-repo-runner/test/cloud-agent-spawn-argv-matrix.test.ts` runs, THEN every agent in `CLOUD_AGENT_MATRIX` produces 9+ parameterized passing tests (3 agents × 9 properties ≈ 27 tests minimum). GIVEN `CLOUD_AGENT_MATRIX[0].requiredArgv` has the `--permission-mode` entry removed by hand, WHEN the same vitest command runs, THEN ≥1 test fails with a message containing the literal string `--permission-mode`. GIVEN a developer adds a 4th cloud agent to the matrix as a new row, WHEN the parameterized tests run, THEN the new agent's 9+ tests appear in the output with no other test file changes needed. GIVEN the slow-tier workflow is dispatched on a runner with devin AND claude installed but aider missing, WHEN the workflow runs, THEN it exits 0 (success) with two passing real-spawn assertions and one "aider not installed — skipped" log line. GIVEN the slow-tier workflow runs on a runner with NO agents installed, WHEN dispatched, THEN it exits 77 (autotools skip) with a clear "no cloud agent available" log line, not silently green.
  - **Risk**: low-to-medium. (a) Real-tier flakiness if a cloud agent's API returns non-deterministic stdout — mitigated by matching against `successPatterns` as RegExp arrays, not literal strings. (b) Adding a 4th agent could surface a contract drift in the simulator — that's exactly the kind of regression this task is meant to surface, so it's a feature not a bug. (c) Agent CLIs evolving their required argv post-merge (devin adds a new required flag in 2026.7.x) — the fast tier catches the drift on the next local install, and the matrix update is a one-row edit; this is the SAME failure mode as agentbrew #1021 designed for and is documented in the PR body there as the intended drift-detection mechanism.

- [ ] `cloud-agent-iteration-record-parity-matrix` — for each cloud agent in `CLOUD_AGENT_MATRIX` (depends on `cloud-agent-spawn-argv-regression-matrix`), the iteration record schema (`.minsky/experiment-store/cross-repo/*.jsonl`) produced by a successful spawn is structurally identical across agents — same fields, same types, same verdict vocabulary; a parameterized parity matrix asserts this property so swapping the default cloud agent from claude to devin (or to a future agent) doesn't silently change downstream metric pipelines (stability, ship-rate, walker-drains)
  - **ID**: cloud-agent-iteration-record-parity-matrix
  - **Tags**: p0, milestone-m1, rule-3a, rule-4, rule-9, test, regression-suite, agentbrew-parallel
  - **Milestone**: M1
  - **Surfaced-by**: 2026-05-21 cross-repo session. Same trigger as `cloud-agent-spawn-argv-regression-matrix` — the agentbrew (agent × CRUD) parity matrix in PR #1022 proved that the same input passing through different agents/adapters produces structurally identical output. Minsky has the same property requirement on iteration records: every `pnpm stability-number`, every `check-cross-repo-pr-rate.mjs`, every metric collector consumes the `*.jsonl` schema. If a future "devin produces `verdict: pr-opened`, claude produces `verdict: PR_OPENED`" drift lands, the metric pipeline silently misclassifies — and the existing tests don't catch it because they're per-agent, not cross-agent.
  - **Hypothesis**: applying the agentbrew `agent-matrix-parity.test.ts` pattern to minsky's iteration-record surface — for each agent, run a single fixed brief through the spawn pipeline (real or simulated), serialize the iteration record, and assert byte-equivalent shape (modulo agent-name field and timestamps) across agents — will detect schema drift within the same CI run as the regression lands AND will keep every downstream metric pipeline immune to per-agent verdict-string variations.
  - **Success**: (1) `pnpm vitest run novel/cross-repo-runner/test/cloud-agent-iteration-parity-matrix.test.ts` produces ≥6 parameterized passing tests (3 agents × 2+ parity properties); (2) deliberately changing one agent's verdict serialization from `pr-open` to `PR_OPEN` in the adapter causes ≥1 parity test to fail with a "drifted from <reference-agent>" message; (3) every consumer in `scripts/stability-report.mjs` / `scripts/check-cross-repo-pr-rate.mjs` / `scripts/iteration-ship-rate.ts` is updated to import the shared `IterationRecordSchema` from the new fixture module (single source of truth), and `grep -r "verdict ===" scripts/ novel/cross-repo-runner/src/` returns zero hardcoded-string matches outside the schema file.
  - **Pivot**: if the canonical-shape comparison turns out to require >5% of iteration-record fields to be agent-specific (e.g., devin's `pr_url` field is always present but claude's is sometimes synthesized from `gh pr view`), retire the byte-equivalent assertion and pivot to a "every consumer asserts only the agent-agnostic subset" model — the schema file becomes the canonical subset definition, agent-specific fields live in a sibling typed-union. Threshold: ≥5 fields demanding agent-specific handling ⇒ pivot.
  - **Measurement**: `pnpm vitest run novel/cross-repo-runner/test/cloud-agent-iteration-parity-matrix.test.ts --reporter=json | jq '[.testResults[0].testResults[] | select(.status=="passed")] | length'` should be ≥6 on green. Cross-check: `grep -rn 'verdict\s*===\s*[\"'\''][a-z_-]*[\"'\'']' scripts/ novel/cross-repo-runner/src/ | wc -l` should fall to ≤1 (only the schema file itself), down from today's baseline (run the same grep on `main` to capture the pre-task count and assert it falls).
  - **Anchor**: Liskov & Wing *Behavioral Subtyping Using Invariants and Constraints* (Carnegie Mellon SCS Tech Report CMU-CS-99-156, 1999) — the substitutability contract this task pins (any cloud agent must produce a record satisfying the same observable invariants as the reference agent) is the exact behavioral subtyping pattern. Parnas *On the Criteria to Be Used in Decomposing Systems into Modules*, CACM 15(12), 1972 — the schema lives behind a module boundary so consumers can't drift past it. Composes with rule #3a (runtime invariants — this task adds a *write-time* invariant on the record schema, complementing the *read-time* invariants in `runtime-invariants.ts`); rule #4 (everything measurable, everything visible — uniform schema ⇒ uniform metric pipeline). Direct sibling: [agentbrew PR #1022](https://github.example.com/example-org/agentbrew/pull/1022) — same parity property applied to MCP-adapter outputs, 31 parameterized tests catching the same drift class.
  - **Details**: (a) Extract today's `IterationRecord` shape from `novel/cross-repo-runner/src/runner.ts` / `iteration-store.ts` into a single typed schema file `novel/cross-repo-runner/src/iteration-record-schema.ts` — exports `IterationRecordSchema` (zod or hand-rolled), `verdictValues: readonly Verdict[]`, and a `parseIterationRecord(line: string)` helper. (b) Add `novel/cross-repo-runner/test/cloud-agent-iteration-parity-matrix.test.ts`: for each row in `CLOUD_AGENT_MATRIX`, run `simulateAgentSpawn` (from the spawn-argv task) with a canonical brief, capture the iteration record, and assert: same field set, same verdict-value vocabulary, same `pr_url` presence-rule when verdict ∈ ship-class. (c) Migrate every consumer (`stability-report.mjs`, `check-cross-repo-pr-rate.mjs`, `iteration-ship-rate.ts`, `runtime-invariants.ts`) to import from the schema file instead of inlining verdict-string literals.
  - **Files**: `novel/cross-repo-runner/src/iteration-record-schema.ts` (new — single source of truth); `novel/cross-repo-runner/test/cloud-agent-iteration-parity-matrix.test.ts` (new — parity assertions); `novel/cross-repo-runner/src/runner.ts` (refactor — import schema instead of inline string literals); `scripts/stability-report.mjs` / `scripts/check-cross-repo-pr-rate.mjs` / `scripts/iteration-ship-rate.ts` (migrate to import from the schema file); `novel/cross-repo-runner/src/runtime-invariants.ts` (use the schema's `verdictValues` instead of hardcoded list).
  - **Acceptance**: GIVEN the schema file exports `verdictValues`, WHEN every consumer in the Files section imports it, THEN `rg "verdict\s*===\s*[\"'][a-z_-]+[\"']" scripts/ novel/cross-repo-runner/src/` returns ≤1 hit. GIVEN the parity matrix file runs, WHEN every agent's iteration record is captured against the canonical brief, THEN the byte-stable diff between any two agents' records (modulo agent-name and timestamps) is empty. GIVEN one agent's adapter is hand-modified to produce `verdict: PR_OPEN` instead of `pr-open`, WHEN the parity matrix re-runs, THEN ≥1 test fails with a message naming both the drifted agent and the reference agent. GIVEN a new cloud agent is added to `CLOUD_AGENT_MATRIX`, WHEN the parity matrix re-runs, THEN the new agent's records are compared against the reference agent automatically with no test code changes.
  - **Risk**: medium. (a) The reference-agent choice (claude is the proposed reference) bakes in a single agent's quirks — mitigated by reviewing the schema file with the assumption "if we delete claude tomorrow, does the schema still describe reality?". (b) Migrating consumers in lockstep is a coordinated multi-file change — mitigated by landing the schema file first as an additive change, then migrating consumers one at a time. (c) The matrix's "byte-stable modulo agent-name and timestamps" comparison can be brittle when fields have non-deterministic ordering — mitigated by serialising records through a stable JSON canonicalizer (sorted keys) before diffing.

- [ ] `cloud-agent-config-and-host-feature-matrix-audit` — per-`~/.minsky/config.json`-agent × per-cross-repo-runner-feature configuration matrix audit that catches drift between the documented config schema, what the runner ACTUALLY supports, and what the slow-tier real-spawn pipeline can exercise; sibling to agentbrew PR #1023 which applied the same audit pattern to the (agent × non-MCP-sync-surface) matrix
  - **ID**: cloud-agent-config-and-host-feature-matrix-audit
  - **Tags**: p0, milestone-m1, rule-3, rule-8, rule-9, test, regression-suite, agentbrew-parallel
  - **Milestone**: M1
  - **Surfaced-by**: 2026-05-21 cross-repo session. The agentbrew (agent × per-feature) matrix in PR #1023 caught 4 categories of configuration drift (path-schema, carve-out vs delegated classification, expected-surface presence, path-clash). Minsky has the same drift class: `~/.minsky/config.json` declares `cloud_agent: "devin"` but `novel/cross-repo-runner/src/agent-launcher.ts` is what actually implements per-agent spawn args; today these can drift silently. AGENTS.md § "Per-machine agent config" documents the schema but isn't enforced. Adding a 4th cloud agent today requires editing config.json, the launcher, MILESTONES.md, and any number of docs — and getting any one of those wrong silently breaks `--local` or the cloud-agent fallback.
  - **Hypothesis**: applying the agentbrew (agent × per-feature) audit pattern from PR #1023 — a single parameterized test file that loops over every (agent, feature) pair in a fixture matrix and asserts (a) path schema well-formedness, (b) classification consistency between schema and implementation, (c) expected-surface presence, (d) no-clash between agents — to minsky's cloud-agent surface will catch every category of config-implementation drift in a single CI run, reducing time-to-detect from "next 10h daemon run" to "next CI run".
  - **Success**: (1) `pnpm vitest run novel/cross-repo-runner/test/cloud-agent-config-audit-matrix.test.ts` produces ≥20 parameterized passing tests across 4 audit dimensions (path-schema × classification × expected-surface × no-clash) × 3 agents; (2) deliberately removing `aider` from `CLOUD_AGENT_MATRIX` but leaving it in `~/.minsky/config.json`'s schema docs causes ≥1 expected-surface test to fail with a message naming `aider`; (3) deliberately adding a 4th agent to `CLOUD_AGENT_MATRIX` but failing to add a matching row to AGENTS.md § "Agent support matrix" causes ≥1 classification test to fail; (4) zero hardcoded agent names appear outside the matrix file in either AGENTS.md or `agent-launcher.ts` (grep returns only the matrix's own definitions).
  - **Pivot**: if the AGENTS.md parser (which needs to extract the "Agent support matrix" table) proves too brittle to maintain — e.g., minor markdown formatting changes break the parser monthly — pivot to a JSON-sidecar at `novel/cross-repo-runner/src/supported-agents.json` that's the canonical source AND auto-renders the AGENTS.md table via a script. Threshold: ≥2 parser-broke incidents in the first month ⇒ pivot.
  - **Measurement**: `pnpm vitest run novel/cross-repo-runner/test/cloud-agent-config-audit-matrix.test.ts --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | grep -c '✓'` ≥ 20 on green; `rg -n '"(claude|devin|aider)"' bin/minsky novel/cross-repo-runner/src/ AGENTS.md INSTALL.md MILESTONES.md | rg -v 'cloud-agent-matrix\.(ts|json)' | wc -l` ≤ 5 (the only remaining hits should be in docs that legitimately mention the agents by name in user-facing text, not in code).
  - **Anchor**: Parnas *On the Criteria to Be Used in Decomposing Systems into Modules*, CACM 15(12), 1972 — the canonical source for "secret a configuration decision behind one module so consumers can't drift past it"; this task makes `CLOUD_AGENT_MATRIX` that module. Wynne & Hellesøy *The Cucumber Book* (2012) Ch. 1 — same example-table-driven assertions as the previous two tasks in this trio. Composes with rule #3 (test-first — the audit IS the test before implementation drifts); rule #8 (pattern conformance — every supported agent must appear in the conformance index, which this matrix becomes the executable form of). Direct sibling: [agentbrew PR #1023](https://github.example.com/example-org/agentbrew/pull/1023) — same 4-dimension audit pattern (path-schema × carve-out-vs-delegated × expected-surface × no-clash) over 50+ agents, 43 passing tests catching the same drift class.
  - **Details**: (a) `novel/cross-repo-runner/test/cloud-agent-config-audit-matrix.test.ts`: 4 describe blocks. (b) Block 1 "Path/argv schema": for each agent in `CLOUD_AGENT_MATRIX`, assert `requiredArgv` array is non-empty, every entry starts with `--`, no duplicates. (c) Block 2 "Carve-out classification": parse `AGENTS.md` § "Agent support matrix" table, assert every agent in the matrix appears in the table AND every table row corresponds to a `CLOUD_AGENT_MATRIX` entry (no orphans either direction). (d) Block 3 "Expected-surface drift guard": for each agent, pin the documented surfaces (`local_agent` only / `cloud_agent` only / both) and assert it matches `CLOUD_AGENT_MATRIX[row].supports`. (e) Block 4 "No-clash": no two distinct agents share the same `requiredArgv` set verbatim (catches accidental copy-paste in the fixture). (f) Add a `scripts/check-cloud-agent-matrix-drift.mjs` lint that runs the same assertions outside vitest so `pnpm pre-pr-lint --stage=full` can include it.
  - **Files**: `novel/cross-repo-runner/test/cloud-agent-config-audit-matrix.test.ts` (new); `scripts/check-cloud-agent-matrix-drift.mjs` (new lint); `package.json` (wire the lint into `pre-pr-lint`); optional `novel/cross-repo-runner/src/supported-agents.json` if pivot path triggers; AGENTS.md (no edit on green path — table parser is read-only).
  - **Acceptance**: GIVEN the audit matrix file exists, WHEN `pnpm vitest run novel/cross-repo-runner/test/cloud-agent-config-audit-matrix.test.ts` runs, THEN ≥20 parameterized tests pass across the 4 audit dimensions. GIVEN a developer adds a 4th cloud agent to `CLOUD_AGENT_MATRIX` without updating AGENTS.md § "Agent support matrix", WHEN the audit matrix re-runs, THEN ≥1 classification test fails with a message naming the missing AGENTS.md row. GIVEN a developer adds a 4th cloud agent row to AGENTS.md without updating `CLOUD_AGENT_MATRIX`, WHEN the audit matrix re-runs, THEN ≥1 classification test fails with a message naming the orphan AGENTS.md row. GIVEN two agents in `CLOUD_AGENT_MATRIX` are given the same `requiredArgv` set by mistake, WHEN the audit matrix re-runs, THEN ≥1 no-clash test fails with a message naming both agents.
  - **Risk**: low. (a) AGENTS.md table parser brittleness — mitigated by tight regex + a paired test that pins the table format. (b) The audit catches valid drift that operators haven't fixed yet (existing agents that don't have a matrix row) — mitigated by an explicit ALLOWLIST in the audit file for grandfathered cases, identical to the `SHARED_PATH_ALLOWLIST` pattern in agentbrew PR #1023. (c) Adding the 4th audit dimension (no-clash) on top of agentbrew's 3 — this is a Minsky-specific addition that catches the copy-paste failure mode unique to argv contracts (where 2 agents could plausibly share `["--print"]` if not careful).

<!-- 2026-05-24 OPERATOR DIRECTIVE — Path A aggressive cut. Operator was offered Path A (~5-10K LOC final) vs continuing Path C (~30K LOC final) and answered "2" (more aggressive). The 4-stream landscape research + brutal-honest moat audit confirmed 2 of 6 claimed moats are genuinely unique; the other 4 are partial. Path A's 7 deletion phases extend Path C's existing plan. Plan doc: `docs/plans/2026-05-24-path-a-aggressive-cut.md`. -->

- [ ] `path-a-phase-7-cross-repo-runner-shell-rewrite` (@devin-2026-05-24) — replace `novel/cross-repo-runner/` (10.8K LOC TypeScript) with `bin/minsky-run` (~300 lines bash) + `scripts/pick_task.py` (~200 lines Python); same end-user-visible behavior with `--hosts-dir <parent>` round-robin
  - **ID**: path-a-phase-7-cross-repo-runner-shell-rewrite
  - **Tags**: p0, milestone-m1, rule-1, path-a, aggressive-cut, observed-2026-05-24
  - **Milestone**: M1
  - **Competitive-goal**: shrinks Minsky LOC from ~62K → ~52K, moving the 1-FTE-sustainable-budget ratio from 6× over to 5× over. Indirectly improves `cost-per-merged-pr` (scorecard metric) because the bash + Python pair has lower per-iteration context overhead than the 10.8K LOC TypeScript scaffold.
  - **Hypothesis**: cross-repo-runner's 10.8K LOC TypeScript can be replaced by a 300-line bash + 200-line Python combo that runs `openhands solve` per host in round-robin and parses TASKS.md. The TypeScript complexity grew incrementally as adapters multiplied; with OpenHands as the single canonical agent (shipped 2026-05-24), most adapter logic is gone, and a small shell + Python script suffices.
  - **Success**: `bin/minsky-run --hosts-dir ~/apps` completes 1 round-robin pass across ≥3 hosts in <30 minutes, opening ≥1 draft PR per host that touched a task. The bash + python combined LOC is ≤ 600. The existing fixture set in `novel/cross-repo-runner/test/` is rewritten as `tests/minsky-run.bats` and passes. After parity confirmed, `novel/cross-repo-runner/` is deleted.
  - **Pivot**: if the bash rewrite hits >100 hours of integration debugging (suggesting the TypeScript complexity was load-bearing), revert to Path C's "keep cross-repo-runner" plan and stop the aggressive cut at Phase 9 (small-package sweep only).
  - **Measurement**: `test -d novel/cross-repo-runner && echo still-here || echo deleted` returns `deleted`; `wc -l bin/minsky-run scripts/pick_task.py | tail -1 | awk '{print $1}'` ≤ 600; `bats tests/minsky-run.bats` exits 0.
  - **Anchor**: rule #1 (don't reinvent — OpenHands SDK does the agent work; the shell is just the round-robin scheduler); `docs/plans/2026-05-24-path-a-aggressive-cut.md` § Phase 7; operator directive 2026-05-24 "2".
  - **Details**: write `bin/minsky-run` as a single bash file with 5 inline invariant checks (replacing observer's runtime-invariants); write `scripts/pick_task.py` as a single Python file that reads TASKS.md, validates rule-9 fields, returns the top unclaimed task ID. Both files have paired tests. Once green, delete `novel/cross-repo-runner/` and update `package.json` `bin` field.
  - **Files**: `bin/minsky-run` (NEW shell, ~300 lines), `scripts/pick_task.py` (NEW Python, ~200 lines), `tests/minsky-run.bats` (NEW), `tests/pick_task.test.py` (NEW), `novel/cross-repo-runner/` (DELETE entirely after parity).
  - **Acceptance**: GIVEN the bash + Python rewrite ships AND parity tests pass, WHEN `bin/minsky-run --hosts-dir ~/apps --dry-run` runs, THEN it emits the same per-host iteration order the TypeScript version did against the same fixture. GIVEN the new code ships, WHEN `ls novel/cross-repo-runner/ 2>/dev/null`, THEN no output.
  - **Risk**: medium-high. Bash is harder to test than TypeScript; the iteration record format (JSONL) must remain byte-stable since MAPE-K reads it. Mitigation: pin the JSONL schema with a JSON-schema validator in pre-pr-lint.
  - **Progress 2026-05-24/25**: bash + Python side fully shipped across PRs #795-#808 (parity for `parseTasksMd`, `pickHostTask`, `findTask`, the round-robin walker, dynamic timeout, watchdog, brief builder, restart-sentinel; supervisor opt-in via `MINSKY_BASH_RUNNER=1` / `MINSKY_INSTALL_DAEMON_BASH=1`; bash-tests CI gate; 99 Python tests + 23 bats tests; ~700 LOC total under the 600 budget when measured net-of-tests). Only remaining importer of `novel/cross-repo-runner/dist/index.js` outside the package itself is `test/integration/daemon-restart.test.ts`. Deletion gate: operator must run `MINSKY_INSTALL_DAEMON_BASH=1 minsky install-daemon` for ≥1 day, confirm the bash side iterates without regression, then the next agent picks up this task and ships the deletion PR (deletes `novel/cross-repo-runner/`, removes the daemon-restart integration test, ports its restart-sentinel scenario to a bats test if not already covered). The phase-11 sidecar-bootstrap port (PR #807) is the other delete-when-gate-lifts target — same operator smoke unblocks both.

  - **ID**: path-a-phase-8-tick-observer-spec-monitor-inline-fold
  - **Tags**: p0, milestone-m1, rule-1, path-a, aggressive-cut, observed-2026-05-24, blocked-on-phase-7
  - **Milestone**: M1

  - **Hypothesis**: the tick-loop's outer iteration logic + the observer's 5 runtime invariants + the spec-monitor's specification-drift detection are 8K LOC total in TypeScript, but functionally they are: (a) one for-loop, (b) 5 small inline bash functions, (c) one `check-rule-coverage.mjs` lint. Total replacement: ≤ 200 lines.
  - **Success**: all three directories deleted. `bin/minsky-run --self-check` exits 0 with a 1-line summary of each invariant. `scripts/check-rule-coverage.mjs` is wired into `pre-pr-lint --stage=full` and exits 0.

  - **Measurement**: `ls novel/tick-loop novel/observer novel/spec-monitor 2>/dev/null | wc -l` returns 0; `wc -l bin/minsky-run` ≤ 500; `pnpm pre-pr-lint --stage=full | grep -c 'check-rule-coverage'` ≥ 1.
  - **Anchor**: rule #1; `docs/plans/2026-05-24-path-a-aggressive-cut.md` § Phase 8.
  - **Details**: depends on Phase 7 (`bin/minsky-run` must exist first). Move the outer iteration logic into the bash loop; map each TypeScript observer invariant to a bash function; convert the spec-monitor to a single pre-pr-lint check.
  - **Files**: `bin/minsky-run` (extend), `scripts/check-rule-coverage.mjs` (NEW), `novel/tick-loop/`, `novel/observer/`, `novel/spec-monitor/` (DELETE all three).
  - **Acceptance**: see Phase 7 acceptance template; same shape applied to each of the three deletions.
  - **Risk**: medium. The cross-package handoffs (observer notifies tick-loop; spec-monitor advises observer) become inline; some subtle integration behavior may be lost. Mitigation: keep the test fixtures from each deleted package and rewrite as parity tests against the inlined version.

  - **Acceptance**: each sub-PR's parity test passes; the operator confirms `minsky watch` still surfaces useful information after `novel/tui/` deletion.
