- [ ] `path-a-phase-13-identity-promotion` — rewrite README.md TL;DR + vision.md § "What Minsky is" for the post-aggressive-cut identity; update Pattern conformance index for deleted packages; add `minsky-discipline-pack` to agentbrew catalog
  - **ID**: path-a-phase-13-identity-promotion
  - **Tags**: p0, milestone-m1, rule-1, path-a, aggressive-cut, observed-2026-05-24, blocked-on-phases-7-12
  - **Milestone**: M1
  - **Competitive-goal**: doesn't shrink LOC directly, but lowers the operator's per-month maintenance ceiling (the headline value of the whole Path A cut). User-facing capability against scorecard metrics (`swe-bench-verified-resolve-rate`, `autonomous-merge-rate`, `mean-autonomous-merge-latency`) is unchanged because OpenHands is the runtime in both pre- and post-cut states.
  - **Hypothesis**: after the aggressive cut completes, Minsky's identity has shifted from "70K-LOC integration distribution" to "5-10K-LOC discipline pack running on OpenHands". The user-facing capability ("24/7 self-improving code factory") is unchanged, but the surface area is 85% smaller. The README + vision.md need to reflect the new identity so newcomers don't expect 70K LOC of TypeScript.
  - **Success**: README TL;DR reads as a discipline-pack story (constitutional rules + MAPE-K substrate on OpenHands), not as a runtime story. `vision.md § "What Minsky is"` matches. Pattern conformance index has no orphan rows (deleted packages → no row). `agentbrew install minsky-discipline-pack` works.
  - **Pivot**: trivial — this is docs.
  - **Measurement**: `grep -c "plug-and-play repo transformer" README.md vision.md` returns 0; `grep -c "discipline pack" README.md vision.md` returns ≥ 2.
  - **Anchor**: rule #1; `docs/plans/2026-05-24-path-a-aggressive-cut.md` § Phase 13.
  - **Details**: blocked on Phases 7-12 completing. Final cleanup PR.
  - **Files**: `README.md`, `vision.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `INSTALL.md`, the agentbrew catalog source file (add `minsky-discipline-pack` source entry).
  - **Acceptance**: a stranger reading only README.md understands that Minsky = discipline pack on OpenHands, not a 70K-LOC TypeScript codebase.
  - **Risk**: low.

- [ ] `phase-7b-delete-cross-repo-runner-multistep` — staged deletion of `novel/cross-repo-runner/` (~4.2K LOC + 6 dependent production files + 2 integration tests). First half of the Phase 7b/11b deletion; tick-loop's deletion follows as a sibling task once this lands clean
  - **ID**: phase-7b-delete-cross-repo-runner-multistep
  - **Tags**: p0, milestone-m1, path-a, aggressive-cut, deletion, observed-2026-05-25
  - **Milestone**: M1
  - **Competitive-goal**: moves `path-a-loc-novel-tree` from 31,234 → ~27K LOC (first ~4.2K cut). Closes the "ready-to-delete but not deleted" anti-pattern by actually shipping the deletion. The 2026-05-25 attempt surfaced that the deletion is NOT a single-PR job — there are 6 production files that reference cross-repo-runner outside the deleted tree.
  - **Hypothesis**: PR #875's live smoke against minsky-on-itself proved the bash skeleton (`bin/minsky-run.sh`) handles end-to-end iteration. So `novel/cross-repo-runner/` IS deletable in principle. But a single-PR `git rm -rf novel/cross-repo-runner/` attempt (2026-05-25 session) found 6 production files referencing it (`novel/adapters/agent-runtime-openhands/src/spawner.ts`, `scripts/minsky-benchmark.mjs`, `scripts/lib/iteration-ship-rate.mjs`, `scripts/check-cross-repo-pr-rate.mjs`, `scripts/post-merge-auto-install.test.mjs`, `scripts/run-pre-pr-lint-stack.mjs`) + 2 integration tests (`test/integration/daemon-restart.test.ts`, `test/integration/proactive-healing-regressions.test.ts`) + 1 tsconfig path + 1 threat-model-section allowlist entry + 1 dashboard-metric-ids.json entry. Pre-registration: post-deletion, `pnpm typecheck && pnpm test` are both green AND `bash bin/minsky --once $MINSKY_REPO` produces a non-stale ledger entry. Falsifiable: if the 6 dependent files cannot be migrated to the bash skeleton cleanly (i.e. they need the TS code paths the bash runner doesn't replicate), the deletion blocks here and we file a parity-gap task for whichever surface is missing.
  - **Success**: (1) `git rev-parse HEAD:novel/cross-repo-runner` returns "fatal: Path '...' does not exist". (2) `pnpm typecheck` green. (3) `pnpm test` green (or any newly-failing tests are explicitly deleted as no-longer-applicable). (4) `bash bin/minsky --once $MINSKY_REPO` exits 0 with non-stale ledger row written. (5) `path-a-loc-novel-tree` metric drops from 31,234 → ~27K.
  - **Pivot**: if the 6 dependent files reveal a parity gap (the bash skeleton misses an API the TS runner exposed), STOP and file a `bash-runner-parity-gap-<surface>` P0 task. Don't paper over the gap with a stub. The whole point is to delete, not coexist.
  - **Measurement**: `fd -e ts -e tsx --type f . novel/ --exclude '*.test.*' | xargs wc -l | tail -1 | awk '{print $1}'` returns ≤27,500 after deletion. AND `bash bin/minsky --once $MINSKY_REPO 2>&1 | grep -c "verdict:"` returns 1 (the post-deletion smoke writes a fresh ledger). AND `pnpm typecheck && pnpm test` both exit 0.
  - **Anchor**: 2026-05-25 single-PR attempt (this session) — the naive `git rm -rf novel/cross-repo-runner/` revealed 6+ dependent files. The right shape is multi-step: (a) migrate / delete each dependent in its own PR, (b) THEN delete the package. PR #876's attempt reverted; this task captures the real plan; `docs/plans/2026-05-24-path-a-aggressive-cut.md` § "Phase 7b"; rule #2 (the bash skeleton IS the canonical iteration surface; the TS runner is the soon-to-be-deleted shim it replaces); Hyrum's Law (every observable property of an interface becomes load-bearing).
  - **Details**: Sequential PR plan (each is a separate PR that lands AND tests pass before the next starts):
    - **Step 1 ✅ (PR #877)**: Comment-only references to cross-repo-runner cleaned up across 5 files; spawner.ts and ship-rate computation decoupled.
    - **Step 2 ✅ (PR #878)**: `scripts/minsky-benchmark.mjs` migrated from `node novel/cross-repo-runner/bin/minsky-run.mjs` to `bash bin/minsky-run.sh`; 18/18 paired tests green.
    - **Step 3 ✅ (PR #879)**: cloud-agent matrix (`agent-config.ts`) + audit lint + paired unit tests ported to `scripts/lib/cloud-agent-config.mjs` + `scripts/cloud-agent-config-audit-matrix-lint.mjs` + `scripts/lib/cloud-agent-config.test.mjs`. `scripts/run-pre-pr-lint-stack.mjs` + `.github/workflows/ci.yml` updated.
    - **Step 4 ✅ (PR #880)**: Integration tests stripped of TS-runner dependencies — `test/integration/proactive-healing-regressions.test.ts` deleted (3 rule-17 lint tests extracted to `rule-17-proactive-heal-lint.test.ts`); `daemon-restart.test.ts` stripped of runHostLoop helper + auto-restart-on-pull block (kept 20 bin/minsky bash tests); `runtime-paths-coverage.test.ts` stripped of 11 L4 RUNNER_BIN tests (kept 4 L3 bin/minsky tests).
    - **Step 5**: Migrate `scripts/check-brief-pr-instructions.mjs` to read from `scripts/build_brief.py` instead of `novel/cross-repo-runner/src/spawn-plan.ts` (the bash runner uses Python build_brief.py).
    - **Step 6**: Update `tsconfig.json` (remove cross-repo-runner path), `scripts/check-threat-model-section.mjs` (remove from allowlist + adjust count), `distribution/shortcuts/test/dashboard-metric-ids.json` (remove path-a-loc-cross-repo-runner from list).
    - **Step 7**: FINAL — `git rm -rf novel/cross-repo-runner/`. Re-run smoke. Verify scoreboard.
  - **Files**: 8+ production files spread across the steps above. See the live-attempt's `rg` output for the full list.
  - **Touches**: `novel/cross-repo-runner/**` (deletion in step 7); `novel/adapters/agent-runtime-openhands/src/spawner.ts` (step 1); `scripts/*` (steps 2-4); `test/integration/*` (step 5); `tsconfig.json` / `scripts/check-threat-model-section*` / `distribution/shortcuts/test/dashboard-metric-ids.json` (step 6).
  - **Acceptance**: (1) Cross-repo-runner directory is gone. (2) All 6 dependent production files are either rewritten or deleted with rationale. (3) Pnpm typecheck + test green. (4) Live smoke against minsky-on-itself shows fresh ledger row. (5) `path-a-loc-novel-tree` metric drops from 31,234 → ~27K.
  - **Risk**: medium-high. Multi-PR coordination is the risk. Mitigations: each step lands as its own PR + CI gate; each step is independently revertible; the live smoke is the final gate.

- [ ] `phase-11b-delete-tick-loop-multistep` — staged deletion of `novel/tick-loop/` (~17K LOC). Follow-up to `phase-7b-delete-cross-repo-runner-multistep`. Tick-loop has ~50+ dependent files because it's the TS daemon's main surface; deletion is at LEAST a 10-step PR sequence
  - **ID**: phase-11b-delete-tick-loop-multistep
  - **Tags**: p0, milestone-m1, path-a, aggressive-cut, deletion, observed-2026-05-25
  - **Milestone**: M1
  - **Competitive-goal**: moves `path-a-loc-novel-tree` from ~27K → ~10K LOC (second ~17K cut). Lands Path A's stated 5-10K target.
  - **Hypothesis**: tick-loop is the TypeScript daemon's main implementation. It exports `runDaemon`, `MockAnthropicClient`, all the SpawnStrategy / TaskRotationSeam / CtoAuditSeam pluggable surfaces. The bash skeleton has parity for `runDaemon`'s iteration loop. Pre-registration: post-deletion, the launchd/systemd units run the bash supervisor instead of `node novel/tick-loop/bin/minsky.mjs`. Tests that exercise the TS daemon get deleted (the seams they verified are gone). Falsifiable: if the bash skeleton misses a surface ≥1 test depends on AND that test verifies USER-VISIBLE behavior (not internal TS-only structure), the deletion blocks here and we file a parity-gap task.
  - **Unblocked**: 2026-05-25 phase-7b shipped (PRs #877-883); phase-11b can begin.
  - **Success**: (1) `git rev-parse HEAD:novel/tick-loop` returns "fatal: Path '...' does not exist". (2) `package.json` no longer points the `minsky` binstub at `node novel/tick-loop/bin/minsky.mjs`. (3) launchd/systemd unit files invoke `bash distribution/systemd/run-tick-loop.sh` (which calls the bash skeleton). (4) Pnpm typecheck + test green. (5) Live smoke produces fresh ledger. (6) `path-a-loc-novel-tree` ≤10K.
  - **Pivot**: if a tick-loop surface IS load-bearing and not yet ported to bash, file the gap and don't paper over it.
  - **Measurement**: `fd -e ts -e tsx --type f . novel/ --exclude '*.test.*' | xargs wc -l | tail -1 | awk '{print $1}'` returns ≤10,000. AND `bash bin/minsky --once $MINSKY_REPO` produces fresh ledger row. AND `pnpm typecheck && pnpm test` exit 0.
  - **Anchor**: parent task `phase-7b-delete-cross-repo-runner-multistep`'s lessons (multi-PR deletion required); `docs/plans/2026-05-24-path-a-aggressive-cut.md` § Phase 11b; rule #2 (bash skeleton IS the canonical surface); rule #6 (let-it-crash AT the right boundary — the boundary is bash, not TS).
  - **Details**: Each tick-loop API still exported needs a per-PR migration step. Audit confirmed surprisingly few JS-import consumers (3 files: `user-stories/001-loop-runs-overnight.test.ts`, `scripts/self-diagnose.mjs`, `scripts/runany-model-audit.mjs`). Most coupling is via the supervisor unit-files (`distribution/launchd/com.minsky.tick-loop.plist` + `distribution/systemd/run-tick-loop.sh`) that invoke `node novel/tick-loop/dist/daemon.js`. Plan:
    - **Step 1 (in-progress, this PR)**: Port `MODEL_CATALOG` + `validateModelCatalog` to `scripts/lib/model-catalog.mjs`. Updates `scripts/self-diagnose.mjs` import. Same 20 paired tests.
    - **Step 2**: Port `decideRunAnyProvider` + `pickStrategicModel` chain to `scripts/lib/` so `scripts/runany-model-audit.mjs` decouples from `@minsky/tick-loop`.
    - **Step 3**: Migrate or delete `user-stories/001-loop-runs-overnight.test.ts` (the user story acceptance test for the TS daemon's overnight loop; bash skeleton has equivalent via launchd KeepAlive).
    - **Step 4**: Flip the `minsky` binstub in `package.json` from `node novel/tick-loop/bin/minsky.mjs` to `bash bin/minsky` (the bash shim).
    - **Step 5**: Switch `distribution/systemd/run-tick-loop.sh` + `distribution/launchd/com.minsky.tick-loop.plist` to invoke `bash bin/minsky-run.sh` instead of `node dist/daemon.js`. The single load-bearing migration.
    - **Step 6**: Strip the remaining lint-side comment / doc references to `novel/tick-loop/` across `scripts/check-*.mjs` (no actual code dependencies; surface cleanup only).
    - **Step 7**: Metadata decouple — `tsconfig.json`, `vitest.config.ts` alias, `knip.json` entry, `scripts/check-threat-model-section.mjs` allowlist, `distribution/shortcuts/test/dashboard-metric-ids.json` (drop `path-a-loc-tick-loop` once deletion lands), `novel/dashboard-web/src/metrics.ts` row, `scripts/collect-metrics.mjs` collector.
    - **Step 8**: Final — `git rm -rf novel/tick-loop/`. Re-run smoke. Verify scoreboard.
  - **Files**: ~50+ files across `novel/tick-loop/**`, `package.json` (binstub), `distribution/launchd/com.minsky.tick-loop.plist`, `distribution/systemd/minsky-tick-loop.service`, `distribution/systemd/run-tick-loop.sh`, `test/integration/*`, `scripts/*`, `user-stories/*.test.ts`.
  - **Touches**: `novel/tick-loop/**`, `package.json`, `distribution/**`, `test/integration/**`, `scripts/**`, `user-stories/**`.
  - **Acceptance**: (1) tick-loop directory is gone. (2) `minsky` binstub invokes bash skeleton. (3) launchd/systemd units use bash. (4) Pnpm typecheck + test green. (5) Live smoke fresh ledger. (6) Scoreboard ≤10K.
  - **Risk**: high. Multi-month effort if done conservatively. Mitigations: each migration is its own PR; the launchd/systemd flip is reversible via a single env var; revert path is `git revert` of the final deletion PR.

- [ ] `daemon-silent-on-claude-account-rate-limit` — `claude --print` exits 1 with `You've hit your limit · resets <date>` when the operator's account is rate-limit-exhausted; minsky's daemon does NOT parse this signal, doesn't transition to `budget-paused`, doesn't notify the operator, doesn't sleep until reset — it just keeps emitting `iteration.status=failed, provider=""` forever, burning machine cycles and obscuring the actual root cause behind the symptom "spawn failed"
  - **ID**: daemon-silent-on-claude-account-rate-limit
  - **Competitive-goal**: drives `unattended-iteration-rate` toward 0.95 — competitors silently retry on provider rate-limits (busy-loops in OpenHands, Aider, Devin queue). Minsky pausing-with-notify on parsed exhaustion signal is observable budget-aware operation, closes the silent-retry-forever gap.
  - **Tags**: p0, milestone-m1, m1-1, rule-1, rule-6, observability, budget-guard, observed-2026-05-26, blocker, anti-stub-marker
  - **Milestone**: M1
  - **Surfaced-by**: 2026-05-26 operator session — ran `pnpm minsky:setup`, watched first iteration fail. Strategic router span: `{kind:"strategic-router","agent":"local","reason":"tier-3 local qualifies","weekly":0.000}`. Iteration: `{"iteration.status":"failed","iteration.provider":""}`. When I ran `echo test | claude --print` directly on this machine, output was: `You've hit your limit · resets May 31 at 8pm (America/Toronto)` (exit 1). So `weekly=0.00` was NOT a cold-start observation — it was the `MaciekTokenMonitor` correctly observing that the operator's weekly Claude window is exhausted. The strategic router did the right thing routing to local. The bug: (a) local has no backend wired AND (b) the daemon doesn't surface "claude exhausted until <reset>" anywhere — no ntfy push, no budget-pause supervisor state, no log line, no nothing. The operator only finds out by running `claude --print` themselves. Connect to M1.1 (90% stability target): with claude exhausted, the daemon spends 100% of iterations on silent spawn-failure until reset. **No amount of work in the cwd can heal this — it's account-level.**
  - **Hypothesis**: spawn the `claude --print` child with stderr captured; if stderr matches `/You've hit your limit · resets (.+)/`, the spawn handler emits a structured `iteration.failure_reason: "claude-account-rate-limit"` field with the parsed reset time, the supervisor transitions to a new `budget-paused-claude` state, the ntfy notifier (if wired) pushes one alert ("Claude exhausted until <reset>; minsky paused"), and the tick-loop sleeps until `reset_time - now` instead of busy-looping every 5 min. After fix: an operator who lets minsky run through a rate-limit transition sees ONE clear log line + ONE push notification, the daemon stops burning cycles, and `pnpm minsky:status` shows the supervisor in `budget-paused-claude` (not `running`) so it's mechanically visible.
  - **Success**: (1) Live-fire reproduction: trigger the rate-limit by running 5+ concurrent `claude --print` invocations; observe the daemon catches the stderr signal within 1 iteration; (2) `tail .minsky/tick-loop.out.log | grep budget-paused-claude` returns a line; (3) `pnpm minsky:status` shows `budget-paused-claude` state with the parsed reset time; (4) the tick-loop sleeps until reset (verified via no new iteration spans between trigger and reset); (5) when reset time passes, the daemon auto-resumes WITHOUT operator intervention (single retry tests the wall).
  - **Pivot**: if parsing claude's stderr is fragile (Anthropic changes the message string and our regex breaks silently), the failure mode is "we stop catching the signal and silently busy-loop again." Pivot trigger: ≥1 operator report of "minsky kept running through a rate-limit" within 30 days of rollout. Pivot to: don't parse — implement a separate `canMintAccessToken` probe (a 1-token `claude --print` probe at boot + every 10 min) that returns a structured `{exhausted, resetAt}` and feed THAT into the budget-guard. The probe is fragile in a different way (Anthropic could change the limit-error wire shape) but at least separates the signal from the per-iteration spawn path.
  - **Measurement**: `tail -200 ~/.minsky/tick-loop.out.log | grep -E "budget-paused-claude|hit your limit" | wc -l | awk '$1 > 0 {print "PASS"} $1 == 0 {print "FAIL"}'`
  - **Anchor**: Rule #1 (loud-crash > silent failure — a rate-limit exhausted account IS a loud signal that the system should propagate, not absorb). Rule #6 (handled-locally — the spawn handler is the right boundary for parsing the rate-limit signal). Beyer SRE 2016 Ch. 4 (SLI/SLO — minsky's stability SLO can't be 90% if budget exhaustion silently zeros it). MILESTONES.md M1.1 (the 90% threshold assumes minsky can DETECT what's wrong; a silent budget-exhausted loop is worse than a crash because it lies).
  - **Details**: (a) Modify the `claude --print` spawn invocation in `novel/tick-loop/src/llm-invocation.ts` (or wherever `buildClaudePrintInvocation` runs) to capture stderr and pattern-match `/You've hit your limit · resets (.+)/`. (b) On match, emit a new span `tick-loop.budget-paused-claude` with `{reset_at, parsed_from_stderr}` AND return a structured outcome `{verdict: "budget-paused-claude", resetAt}` instead of `spawn-failed`. (c) The supervisor's iteration boundary (in `novel/cross-repo-runner/src/host-loop.ts`) detects this verdict and transitions the supervisor state to `budget-paused-claude`; the strategic-router gates subsequent picks (returns `kind: "budget-paused"` with no spawn). (d) The tick-loop's sleep computation factors in the resetAt: `sleep = max(MINSKY_TICK_INTERVAL_MS, resetAt - now)` so the daemon naturally idles until reset. (e) If ntfy is wired, push ONE notification per transition (edge-triggered debounce — don't spam). (f) Paired tests for: regex parsing variants (with/without timezone string, different date formats Anthropic might emit), state transition idempotency (multiple consecutive limits don't re-notify), reset-time-elapsed auto-resume.
  - **Files**: `novel/tick-loop/src/llm-invocation.ts` (stderr capture + rate-limit pattern match), `novel/tick-loop/src/budget-paused-claude.ts` (new — pure state-transition function), `novel/tick-loop/src/budget-paused-claude.test.ts` (new), `novel/cross-repo-runner/src/host-loop.ts` (recognize `budget-paused-claude` verdict), `novel/cross-repo-runner/src/host-loop.test.ts` (test the verdict), `bin/tick-loop.mjs` (wire the resetAt into sleep computation).
  - **Acceptance**: (1) `pnpm vitest run novel/tick-loop/src/budget-paused-claude.test.ts` passes; (2) `pnpm typecheck` clean; (3) Live-fire: when the OPERATOR's claude session is rate-limited (verified by `echo x | claude --print` returning the "hit your limit" message), `pnpm minsky:setup` running for ≥1 iteration produces a `budget-paused-claude` span in the log within 5 min, the supervisor's PID exits cleanly OR the tick-loop visibly idles (no new strategic-pick spans for the duration until reset), and `pnpm minsky:status` reflects the state; (4) when the operator's claude session recovers (or the reset wall passes), the next tick fires a real iteration without operator intervention.

- [ ] `devin-per-worker-isolation-primitive` — claude has `--worktree <name>` which auto-creates an isolated git worktree per session; devin has no equivalent today. When the daemon scales to ≥2 workers (`MINSKY_AUTO_SCALE_WORKERS=1`, max 5) and cloud_agent=devin, all workers iterate against the shared main checkout — concurrent commits could collide
  - **ID**: devin-per-worker-isolation-primitive
  - **Competitive-goal**: drives `agent-parity-coverage` toward 1.00 (M1.9) — Claude Code ships `--worktree`; Devin doesn't. Minsky's own worktree primitive on top makes M1.9 (works from Claude, Devin, local) verifiable end-to-end with the same parallel-iteration semantics regardless of CLI backend.
  - **Tags**: p2, milestone-m1, m1-1, devin-support, parallel-workers, observed-2026-05-26
  - **Milestone**: M1
  - **Surfaced-by**: 2026-05-26 — operator switched to devin (`MINSKY_CLOUD_AGENT=devin`). Auto-scale is on by default (initial=1, max=5). With cloud_agent=devin, the worker-config's `--worktree` flag is stripped (per `claude-args-for-worker-should-be-cloud-agent-aware`), so all workers share the main checkout. The worker-claim layer prevents two workers from picking the SAME task, but two workers picking DIFFERENT tasks both run in the same git checkout → race conditions in `git status`, branch switches, commit ordering.
  - **Hypothesis**: extend `worker-config.ts` with `ensureDevinWorktree(taskId)` — a pure helper that runs `git worktree add daemon-<workerId>-<taskId>` against the main checkout BEFORE devin spawn, then passes `cwd: <worktree-path>` to `buildDevinPrintInvocation`. Mirror the pattern of `ensureLocalWorktree` (already in `bin/tick-loop.mjs`). On iteration completion, `git worktree remove` cleans up. Falsifiable: with `MINSKY_AUTO_SCALE_WORKERS=1` + 2 active workers + cloud_agent=devin, the daemon log shows each worker spawning devin with a distinct cwd path.
  - **Success**: (1) Two concurrent devin workers don't collide on `git status` / branch state; (2) Each worker's PR lands cleanly against its dedicated worktree-branch; (3) Removed worktrees don't leave stale entries in `.git/worktrees/`; (4) `git worktree list` after iteration completion shows only the main checkout (no `daemon-N-<task>` leftovers).
  - **Pivot**: if `git worktree add` is too slow at iteration scale (>3s p95 worktree creation), gate the multi-worker devin path behind `MINSKY_AUTO_SCALE_WORKERS=0` and run single-worker devin only. Threshold: ≥1 worker spawn took >5s on worktree creation → pivot.
  - **Measurement**: `git worktree list | grep -c "daemon-" | awk '$1 == 0 {print "PASS"} $1 > 0 {print "FAIL: " $1 " orphaned worktrees"}'`
  - **Anchor**: MILESTONES.md M1.9 (works from Claude Code, Devin, and local models — parity means devin needs the same per-worker isolation claude got from `--worktree`). Rule #2 (single source of truth — worktree-creation logic lives in one helper, not scattered).
  - **Details**: (a) Add `ensureDevinWorktree(taskId, workerId)` to `bin/tick-loop.mjs` (mirrors `ensureLocalWorktree`). (b) When `cloud_agent === "devin"` AND `workerConfig !== undefined`, call `ensureDevinWorktree` before spawn; pass cwd to `buildDevinPrintInvocation`. (c) `removeDevinWorktree(name)` cleanup on iteration completion (in the per-iteration finally block, similar to local). (d) Test: spawn 2 workers with cloud_agent=devin, assert each gets a distinct cwd, both worktrees removed at completion.
  - **Files**: `novel/tick-loop/bin/tick-loop.mjs` (add ensureDevinWorktree + removeDevinWorktree functions and call them in the devin invocation path), `novel/tick-loop/test/devin-worktree-isolation.test.mjs` (new — chaos test for the 2-worker collision case).
  - **Acceptance**: (1) Test passes; (2) Live-fire: `MINSKY_AUTO_SCALE_WORKERS=1`, observe 2 concurrent workers spawning devin with distinct cwds (visible in spawn span); (3) After 5 iterations, `git worktree list` shows only the main checkout (no leftover daemon-N-<task> worktrees).

- [ ] `watchdog-timeout-kills-productive-devin` — the 900s (15min) watchdog SIGKILLs devin mid-work; devin iterations take 5-6min when productive but the watchdog fires on slow iterations, wasting the entire iteration
  - **ID**: watchdog-timeout-kills-productive-devin
  - **Tags**: p0, milestone-m1, devin, watchdog, reliability
  - **Milestone**: M1
  - **Competitive-goal**: a killed productive iteration wastes 15min of devin time with zero output — directly regresses cost-per-PR and stability.
  - **Surfaced-by**: 2026-05-18 live daemon: one `spawn-failed` at exactly 900014ms (the 900s watchdog) while the other iterations completed in 335-382s. The watchdog killed what was likely a productive-but-slow iteration.
  - **Details**: the default `claudePrintTimeoutMs` is 900_000 (15 min), set in `tick-loop.mjs:340`. But minsky-run.mjs (the cross-repo runner) doesn't use the tick-loop's spawn strategy — it has its own spawn path. Check which timeout the cross-repo runner uses and whether it's too aggressive for devin. Devin may legitimately take >15min on complex tasks. The existing P2 task `worker-watchdog-scale-by-pinned-model-latency` addresses this for the tick-loop but not for the cross-repo runner.
  - **Files**: `novel/cross-repo-runner/bin/minsky-run.mjs` (spawn timeout configuration), `novel/tick-loop/bin/tick-loop.mjs` (reference — already has `MINSKY_CLAUDE_PRINT_TIMEOUT_MS` env override)
  - **Hypothesis**: raising the cross-repo runner's spawn timeout to 1800s (30min) eliminates watchdog-killed productive iterations while still catching truly stuck spawns.
  - **Success**: 0 `spawn-failed` at exactly 900s over 10 consecutive iterations; productive iterations that take 10-20min complete normally.
  - **Pivot**: if 30min is too long for stuck detection, implement a "progress watchdog" that checks whether the spawn has produced any stdout in the last 5min instead of a fixed wall-clock timeout.
  - **Measurement**: `jq 'select(.verdict=="spawn-failed") | .notes' .minsky/experiment-store/cross-repo/*.jsonl | grep -c '900'` → 0 (was: 1).
  - **Anchor**: 2026-05-18 live daemon (900014ms spawn-failed). Existing P2 `worker-watchdog-scale-by-pinned-model-latency`.

- [ ] `minsky-config-json-support-local-llm-pref` — `~/.minsky/config.json` supports `cloud_agent` + `cloud_agent_model` as documented persistent keys, but NOT `local_llm_enabled`, `strategic_router_enabled`, or `cloud_agent_fallback`. So an operator who wants "use devin, fall back to local" has to add `MINSKY_LOCAL_LLM=1` to the rendered launchd plist, which gets wiped on the next `pnpm minsky:setup` regeneration. Per-machine preferences should live in ONE place
  - **ID**: minsky-config-json-support-local-llm-pref
  - **Competitive-goal**: drives `unattended-iteration-rate` toward 0.95 — competitor agents' per-machine config (Aider .aiderconfig, Cline settings.json) lives in one file. Minsky splitting cloud_agent (config.json) vs local_llm (rendered plist env) is a UX regression vs the field; closing it removes a friction point operators see in head-to-head trials.
  - **Tags**: p1, milestone-m1, m1-1, config-as-code, rule-2, persistence, observed-2026-05-26
  - **Milestone**: M1
  - **Surfaced-by**: 2026-05-26 operator directive — "update minsky on this machine so it always uses devin... and falls back to local model." Devin part went into `cloud_agent` (existing persistent key). Local-fallback part has NO persistent key — only the env var `MINSKY_LOCAL_LLM=1` exists. The operator's preference is now split: cloud_agent in config.json, local_llm in rendered plist (transient).
  - **Hypothesis**: extend `MinskyConfig` schema with three optional booleans: `local_llm_enabled?: boolean`, `strategic_router_enabled?: boolean`, `cloud_agent_fallback?: "local" | "claude" | "none"`. Each maps 1:1 to the equivalent env var (env wins on conflict). `bin/tick-loop.mjs` reads config.json at startup and sets the env vars BEFORE the rest of the daemon code runs, so the downstream env-var reads continue to work without refactor. Falsifiable: operator writes `{local_llm_enabled: true}` to config.json, restarts daemon, observes `[tick-loop] local-llm wired (...)` log line.
  - **Success**: (1) operator's complete preference fits in `~/.minsky/config.json` — no rendered-plist edits needed; (2) `pnpm minsky:setup` regeneration preserves operator preferences (the plist template doesn't carry per-machine state); (3) the three new config keys are documented in the README + an example config.json shipped at `docs/example-config.json`.
  - **Pivot**: if the config-to-env translation layer at supervisor startup proves fragile (config drift between read time and downstream module load), gate the new keys behind a `MINSKY_CONFIG_AUTOLOAD=1` opt-in env var for the first 30 days of rollout. Threshold: ≥1 operator report of "I set the config but the daemon ignored it" → keep the auto-load gated.
  - **Measurement**: `node -e 'const fs=await import("fs"); fs.writeFileSync(process.env.HOME + "/.minsky/config.json", JSON.stringify({...JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(process.env.HOME+"/.minsky/config.json","utf8")), local_llm_enabled: true})); ' && pnpm minsky:setup && sleep 5 && grep -c "local-llm wired" <minsky-repo>/.minsky/tick-loop.out.log | awk '$1 > 0 {print "PASS"}'`
  - **Anchor**: Rule #2 (single source of truth — config.json IS that source). MILESTONES.md M1.3 (one-command install — install writes ONE file the operator can edit to express ALL their preferences).
  - **Details**: (a) Extend `MinskyConfig` interface in `bin/minsky-run.mjs` (or extract to a shared types file) with the three optional booleans. (b) Add a small startup block in `bin/tick-loop.mjs` that reads config.json, translates the booleans to env vars `MINSKY_LOCAL_LLM` / `MINSKY_STRATEGIC_ROUTER` / `MINSKY_CLOUD_AGENT_FALLBACK`, setting them on `process.env` BEFORE the existing env-var reads downstream. (c) Env vars still take precedence (rule #2 — env override is documented one-session lever). (d) Document in README's config section + ship `docs/example-config.json`.
  - **Files**: `novel/cross-repo-runner/bin/minsky-run.mjs` (extend MinskyConfig type), `novel/tick-loop/bin/tick-loop.mjs` (startup config-to-env block), `docs/example-config.json` (new), `README.md` (config section update).
  - **Acceptance**: (1) Operator writes `{local_llm_enabled: true}` to config.json, restarts daemon, sees the local-llm-wired log line; (2) Existing operators with no config.json continue working unchanged; (3) `MINSKY_LOCAL_LLM=0` env still overrides `local_llm_enabled: true` (env wins).

- [ ] `competitor-deep-dive-wave-2026-05-24` — umbrella task tracking the 35-competitor deep-research wave (Five Pivot Questions framework applied to every existing `competitors/<id>.md` + every new ≥500-star OSS autonomous-coding agent); decision-relevant findings route to `ask_human.md`
  - **ID**: competitor-deep-dive-wave-2026-05-24
  - **Competitive-goal**: drives `published-readings-corpus-coverage` toward 100% (M1.10) — the umbrella wave hits every Tier-S/A entry in one pass so the corpus-refresh-check supervisor stops auto-filing single-competitor tasks; replaces drip-fed maintenance with a one-shot scorecard refresh.
  - **Tags**: p0, milestone-m1, rule-1, competitive, umbrella, operator-directive, observed-2026-05-24
  - **Milestone**: M1
  - **Surfaced-by**: operator directive 2026-05-24 after reviewing the 4-stream autonomous-coding-landscape research — "Next I need full deepest information about all of these similar to minsky tools. For each one of them that has at least 500 stars on github (include dead repos for analysis on what went wrong) create a P0 task in minsky to fully research it. Research must be written to competitors docs, and questions for pivoting should be put to a file for human communication."
  - **Hypothesis**: completing this 35-competitor wave produces ≥3 vision-changing findings (operator-resolved via ask_human.md), ≥10 architectural patterns we should absorb (filed as research-finding-* P3 tasks via the skill's Phase 6), and ≥1 "cuttable Minsky surface" (Minsky surface % to drop) per existing competitors/<id>.md row in the moats table. The wave operationalizes rule #1 (don't reinvent) by forcing a per-competitor decision on every Minsky surface.
  - **Success**: (a) Every sub-task `competitor-deepen-<id>` and `competitor-add-<id>` below has its `competitors/<id>.md` containing a populated `## Five pivot questions` section; (b) ≥1 Q-block in `ask_human.md` per vision-changing finding (or explicit "no vision-changing finding" answer in §3 of each competitor's doc); (c) the wave-summary row in `vision.md § Strategic moats` updates the "% of Minsky surface possibly cuttable" headline number; (d) `bin/minsky competitive --json | jq '.competitors | length'` ≥ 8 (corpus grew from the 6 current entries — many of these competitors publish primary metrics worth adding).
  - **Pivot**: if the first 5 deepens (`aider`, `cline`, `openhands`, `cursor-agent`, `swe-agent`) all produce "no vision-changing finding", abandon the remaining 30 — the wave's signal-to-noise is too low, and operator time is better spent shipping Minsky's existing differentiation. Threshold: 0 vision-changing findings + 0 cuttable-surface findings across the first 5 ⇒ stop, demote remaining 30 to P3 background.
  - **Measurement**: `ls competitors/*.md | wc -l` ≥ 35 (target file count); `grep -l '## Five pivot questions' competitors/*.md | wc -l` ≥ 35 (every doc has the new section); `grep -c '^### Q' ask_human.md` reflects the vision-threat count; `gh api search/repositories?q=... | jq` confirms star counts at time of research (anti-stale-data check).
  - **Anchor**: rule #1 (don't reinvent the wheel — research the existing field before building); rule #5 (theoretical grounding — every competitor cites their own primary source); `~/.claude/skills/competitor-research/SKILL.md` Phase 7 (the framework being applied here); 2026-05-24 4-stream research output (the seed list); operator directive 2026-05-24.
  - **Details**: track 35 sub-tasks below. **Existing-doc deepens** (17): aider, cline, claude-agent-sdk, codex-cli, composio-ao, crewai, cursor-agent, devin, goose, langgraph, metagpt, microsoft-agent-framework, omc, openhands, ralph-wiggum-official, swe-agent, agentless. **Net-new ≥500-★ adds** (17): autogpt, open-interpreter, gpt-engineer, continue-dev, smolagents, roo-code, babyagi, devika, pydantic-ai, plandex, smol-developer, pr-agent, sweep, refact-ai, auto-code-rover, continuous-claude, factory. **One under-500-★ exception flagged for operator decision via ask_human.md**: live-swe-agent (396★, 79.2% SWE-bench Verified SoTA Nov 2025) — file `competitor-add-live-swe-agent` only if the operator answers "include" on that Q.
  - **Files**: `competitors/<id>.md` × 35, `ask_human.md`, optional `novel/competitive-benchmark/src/competitors.ts` (per-competitor — only if vendor publishes a primary metric), `vision.md § Strategic moats` (headline-number update at wave completion).
  - **Acceptance**: (a) 35 sub-task IDs are closed (block removed from TASKS.md per spec); (b) `ask_human.md` has all answered Q-blocks (or explicit "defer" markers); (c) a wave-summary commit lands titled `competitor-deep-dive-wave-2026-05-24: <N> vision-changing findings, <M> cuttable surfaces, <K> adopted patterns`; (d) the Five Pivot Questions skill phase is preserved in `~/.claude/skills/competitor-research/SKILL.md` (DONE 2026-05-24).
  - **Risk**: medium. (a) Scope-bloat — 35 tasks is a lot. Mitigation: the Pivot above; each sub-task is ~30-60 min of focused work (the existing competitors/<id>.md files mostly need the Five Pivot Questions section appended, not full rewrites). (b) Star-count drift — counts were as of 2026-05-24 via `gh api repos/<owner>/<repo>` and may have shifted by the time the work runs; the picker re-checks in each sub-task's measurement step.

- [ ] `minsky-repo-git-config-bare-misset` — `.git/config` on the operator's minsky checkout has `bare = true`, but the directory contains a working tree with active worktrees (`.git/worktrees/daemon-0-*`, `fix-ci-main`, `fleet-stability`, `minsky-bench`). Every `git status` from the main checkout fails with `fatal: this operation must be run in a work tree`. Daemon self-diagnose flagged it under `git-config-parseable` but proposed the wrong fix (`rm .git/index.lock` — index lock is not the issue). Real fix: one-line `bare = false`. The self-diagnose handler should detect `bare = true` AND working files coexisting, propose the correct fix
  - **ID**: minsky-repo-git-config-bare-misset
  - **Competitive-goal**: drives `mttr-self-heal` p95 toward <5min (M1.13) — competitors' self-diagnose surfaces (Devin's `diagnose`, Cursor's health-check) don't propose actionable fixes for unusual git states; Minsky's self-diagnose that flags `bare=true with worktrees` AND proposes `git config core.bare false` is a measurable diagnose-vs-suggest quality win.
  - **Tags**: p1, milestone-m1, m1-1, observed-2026-05-26, self-diagnose-misdirection
  - **Milestone**: M1
  - **Surfaced-by**: 2026-05-26 — every iteration's self-diagnose emits `git status failed (...fatal: this operation must be run in a work tree)`. Confirmed via `cat <minsky-repo>/.git/config` → `[core] bare = true`. `.git/worktrees/` shows 5 active worktrees (4 daemon-spawned + 3 operator). The daemon's self-diagnose suggested `rm .git/index.lock` (the index isn't locked — `ls .git` shows no `index.lock`). Real fix: flip `bare = false` (or remove the line). Tracked to a likely accidental `git init --bare` or similar tool misconfig at some point in the repo's history.
  - **Hypothesis**: extend the self-diagnose handler in `novel/cross-repo-runner/src/daemon.ts` (or wherever `git-config-parseable` is computed) — when `git status` fails with `fatal: this operation must be run in a work tree`, ALSO probe `git config core.bare` and `ls .git/worktrees/`. If both `bare = true` AND worktree-entries exist, suggest `git config core.bare false` instead of the index-lock fix. The combination is mechanically diagnostic. Falsifiable: simulate the bug on a test fixture (`git init && git config core.bare true && touch a.txt`), run the daemon's self-diagnose, assert the suggested fix is the bare flip — not the index lock.
  - **Success**: (1) Self-diagnose detects bare-misset and proposes the correct fix string; (2) Test fixture in `novel/cross-repo-runner/test/self-diagnose-bare-misset.test.mjs` (new) reproduces both detection AND correct suggestion; (3) Operator runs the suggested `git config core.bare false`, `git status` works, daemon iteration completes normally.
  - **Pivot**: if detecting `bare = true` is too fragile (different git versions emit slightly different error messages), gate the new diagnostic behind a `MINSKY_SELF_DIAGNOSE_BARE_DETECTION=1` env var. Threshold: ≥1 false-positive ("flagged bare-misset when actually a different issue") within 7 days of rollout.
  - **Measurement**: `cd <minsky-repo> && git status --short 2>&1 | head -1 | awk '/fatal/ {print "FAIL"} !/fatal/ {print "PASS"}'`
  - **Anchor**: Rule #1 (loud-crash > silent failure — the daemon DOES emit a self-diagnose finding, but the suggested fix is wrong, so the operator follows the misleading advice and the bug persists). Rule #17 (proactive healing — once the correct fix is detected, the next step is auto-applying it; this task only fixes the diagnosis layer, auto-apply is a follow-up).
  - **Details**: (a) Find the self-diagnose handler that emits `git-config-parseable`. (b) After confirming `git status` fails, probe `git config core.bare` (read-only). (c) If `true`, probe `ls -d .git/worktrees/* 2>/dev/null` for active worktrees. (d) If both signals match, suggest `git config core.bare false` instead of the index-lock fix. (e) Paired tests for: bare=false (no false positive), bare=true with no worktrees (true bare repo — different fix needed), bare=true with worktrees (the bug case — propose correct fix). (f) Don't auto-apply the fix yet — that's a follow-up task once the detection is proven stable.
  - **Files**: `novel/cross-repo-runner/src/daemon.ts` (extend self-diagnose handler — locate via `grep -n git-config-parseable`), `novel/cross-repo-runner/test/self-diagnose-bare-misset.test.mjs` (new — paired tests with fixture worktrees), `~/.minsky/daemon.log` (verify after rollout — new suggestedFix string visible).
  - **Acceptance**: (1) `pnpm vitest run novel/cross-repo-runner/test/self-diagnose-bare-misset.test.mjs` passes; (2) `pnpm typecheck` clean; (3) Operator runs `cat <minsky-repo>/.git/config | grep bare` → sees `bare = true` (today's state); runs `git config core.bare false`; runs `git status` → works; the next iteration's self-diagnose drops the finding (no longer fires); (4) When run against a fresh bare-misset fixture, the daemon's self-diagnose emits a suggestedFix containing `git config core.bare false`.

## P1

- [ ] `launcher-agnostic-feature-parity-chaos-test` — chaos test asserts that two Minsky installs on the same OS, same fixture repo, same model, driven through `INSTALL.md` by two different launcher agents (fake-claude, fake-cursor) produce byte-identical runtime behavior; the only permitted delta is the `agent` string in `~/.minsky/telemetry-consent.json`
  - **ID**: launcher-agnostic-feature-parity-chaos-test
  - **Tags**: p1, milestone-m1, chaos-test, invariant, story-014, operator-directive, observed-2026-05-24
  - **Milestone**: M1
  - **Competitive-goal**: protects moats #1 (daemon-not-framework) and #2 (operator-machine-identity) from `competitors/README.md` — no other orchestrator-tier competitor (CrewAI, AutoGen, LangGraph, MetaGPT, Devin) advertises launcher-agnostic feature parity because they bundle launcher and runtime (Devin = Cognition's CLI + Devbox; CrewAI Enterprise = platform; LangGraph = framework). Minsky's launcher-agnostic install is the only way to honor moats #1+#2 simultaneously; this chaos test catches future PRs that would silently erode either moat. Drives `launcher-agnostic-runtime-divergence-count` toward 0 (iron).
  - **Surfaced-by**: operator 2026-05-24 — "it's crucial to be able to run minsky fully from any agent chat including with claude, and it must have same features no matter where from minsky was called. Ensure this is recorded with user stories, add tasks if needed." User-story 014 (`launcher-agnostic-feature-parity.md`) records the invariant; this task implements the regression test that catches future violations.
  - **Hypothesis**: today the daemon's runtime behavior is *believed* to be launcher-agnostic but not *verified* — there's no CI gate that catches a future PR adding `if (process.env.CLAUDE_CODE) { ... }` to runtime code. A chaos test that runs INSTALL.md from two stubbed launchers against the same fixture and diffs the resulting snapshots (config.json, subcommand set, OpenHands envelope, iteration-log shape) catches launcher-coupling regressions in CI. Falsifiable: post-fix, deliberately inject a launcher branch into `novel/cross-repo-runner/bin/minsky-run.mjs` and confirm the test fails; remove the injection and confirm it passes.
  - **Success**: `pnpm vitest run test/chaos/launcher-agnostic-feature-parity.test.ts` exits 0 on main; deliberate injection of any of {`process.env.CLAUDE_CODE`, `process.env.CURSOR`, `process.env.DEVIN_AGENT`, `process.env.MINSKY_LAUNCHER`} into runtime branching makes the test fail with a per-field diff naming the divergent field. CI gate wires the test into `pnpm pre-pr-lint --stage=full`.
  - **Pivot**: if the chaos test fixture grows beyond 500 LOC OR the two-launcher stub framework requires >1d of maintenance per quarter, simplify to a static-analysis lint (`scripts/check-launcher-agnostic-parity.mjs`) that greps for the forbidden env-var patterns instead of running the full install both ways. Document the simplification in `docs/validated-learnings.md` so future agents understand why we dropped from chaos test to lint.
  - **Measurement**: `pnpm vitest run test/chaos/launcher-agnostic-feature-parity.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | grep -E "PASS|FAIL"` reports `PASS` on main and `FAIL` on the deliberate-injection regression branch.
  - **Anchor**: user-story 014 § Failure modes table (this task is the primary chaos test row); rule #9 (pre-registered HDD); Nygard, M. — *Release It!*, 2nd ed. (Pragmatic Bookshelf 2018) — "test the seams between subsystems with chaos faults" applied to the launcher-runtime seam.
  - **Details**: Vertical slice in 4 sub-deliverables. (1) **Two fake launchers** — `test/fixtures/launchers/fake-claude.mjs` and `test/fixtures/launchers/fake-cursor.mjs`, each is a Node script that simulates "an agent following INSTALL.md" — sets env vars characteristic of that launcher (`CLAUDE_CODE=1` for fake-claude, `CURSOR=1` for fake-cursor), then exec's the install steps. (2) **Fixture repo + install runner** — `test/chaos/launcher-agnostic-feature-parity.test.ts` creates a temp repo, runs each launcher against it, snapshots `~/.minsky/config.json` + `bin/minsky --help` output + the OpenHands spawn envelope (mock-mode) + `.minsky/state.json`. (3) **Diff + allowlist** — diffs the two snapshot trees; the only permitted delta is `telemetry_consent.agent`. Any other delta fails the test with a per-field diff in the assertion. (4) **CI gate** — wire into `pnpm pre-pr-lint --stage=full` via the manifest. Add the new test path to `pre-pr-lint.manifest.json`.
  - **Files**: `test/chaos/launcher-agnostic-feature-parity.test.ts` (new), `test/fixtures/launchers/fake-claude.mjs` (new), `test/fixtures/launchers/fake-cursor.mjs` (new), `scripts/snapshot-minsky-install.mjs` (new — pure snapshotter), `pre-pr-lint.manifest.json` (extend full stage), `user-stories/014-launcher-agnostic-feature-parity.md` (cross-reference once the test ships).
  - **Touches**: `test/chaos/`, `test/fixtures/launchers/`, `scripts/snapshot-minsky-install.mjs`, `pre-pr-lint.manifest.json`, `user-stories/014-launcher-agnostic-feature-parity.md`.
  - **Acceptance**: (1) `pnpm vitest run test/chaos/launcher-agnostic-feature-parity.test.ts` exits 0 on a clean main. (2) Deliberate injection of `if (process.env.CLAUDE_CODE) { config.cloud_agent = "claude"; }` into `novel/cross-repo-runner/bin/minsky-run.mjs` causes the test to fail with an assertion naming `config.cloud_agent` as the divergent field. (3) The test runs in ≤30s wall-clock (fast enough for the full pre-pr-lint stage). (4) `pnpm pre-pr-lint --stage=full` includes the chaos test as a load-bearing gate.
  - **Risk**: low. (a) Fake-launcher stubs may not perfectly simulate real launcher behavior — mitigated by keeping the stubs minimal (just env-var setting + exec'ing install steps); the test's value is in catching env-var-driven branches, not in reproducing every real-agent quirk. (b) The snapshot allowlist (only `telemetry_consent.agent` is permitted to differ) may need extension if a future PR legitimately adds a launcher-recorded field; mitigation: extend the allowlist with a comment justifying the new field.
  - **Note**: This task closes story-014's chaos-test row in the failure-modes table. When it ships, update the row from "filed" to "shipped" and add the test path to the table's "Chaos test" column.

- [ ] `gh-actions-checkout-v4-flaky-auth-failures` — `actions/checkout@v4` intermittently fails with "fatal: could not read Username for github.com: terminal prompts disabled" on random jobs in random PRs; the failure is non-deterministic per-PR and per-job; admin-merges have been required to unblock the autonomous loop
  - **ID**: gh-actions-checkout-v4-flaky-auth-failures
  - **Tags**: p1, scout, ci-cd, gh-actions, flaky, observed-2026-05-23
  - **Competitive-goal**: closes the rule #11 violation ("no flaky metric is load-bearing") that the rollup `ci` GitHub Actions gate currently exhibits. The CI gate IS load-bearing for every PR's merge eligibility, AND the checkout-auth flake makes it intermittently red on non-deterministic jobs — a constitutional violation of rule #11. Resolving the flake restores the rollup `ci` gate to reliable-load-bearing status and removes the operator's recurring need to admin-merge PRs that fail only on infrastructure.
  - **Surfaced-by**: 2026-05-23 — PR #738 had 62 checks pass + 3 fail (`cadence-pivot-threshold`, `rule-2-dep-coverage`, and the rollup `ci` job); both substantive failures were `Run actions/checkout@v4` git-auth errors (run [26325409891](https://github.com/fyodoriv/minsky/actions/runs/26325409891)). The failure pattern recurs across docs-only PRs — `cadence-pivot-threshold` and `rule-2-dep-coverage` have shown the same git-auth pattern in earlier admin-merged PRs (#735 hit one, #736 hit none, #737 hit none; #738 hit 2). The non-determinism — same workflow runs green or red across reruns on identical code — confirms it's a flake, not a config issue.
  - **Details**: investigate root cause. Hypotheses: (a) GHE Actions runner authentication token rotation race when many jobs spawn in parallel; (b) `GITHUB_TOKEN` permission scoping changed on a recent runner image upgrade; (c) flaky DNS resolution against github.com from the runner during the auth handshake; (d) Actions runner cache eviction during the auth step. Mitigation candidates: (1) pin `actions/checkout` to a specific SHA rather than `@v4`; (2) add retry logic via `actions/checkout`'s `retry` config; (3) replace with `actions/checkout@v5` (the next major may have addressed it); (4) use `ssh` URL instead of `https://` for fetch (`with: { ssh-key: ... }`). Verify by re-running PR #738 + #739 + #740's failed jobs and counting the auth-failure rate before/after each mitigation. Operator's existing admin-merge discipline covers the immediate unblock; this task addresses the class fix.
  - **Hypothesis**: pinning `actions/checkout` to v5 or a specific SHA reduces the auth-failure rate from ~2/65 jobs per PR to ≤1/200. Falsifiable: post-fix, track the next 20 PRs for `actions/checkout@*` job-failure rate; if rate stays ≥1/100 jobs, the root cause isn't the action version and the hypothesis is wrong.
  - **Success**: PR-level CI rate of `actions/checkout`-attributed failures drops to ≤1/200 jobs across 20 consecutive PRs; `gh-actions-flake-tracker.mjs` (new) reports the rate weekly in METRICS.md.
  - **Pivot**: if pinning to a SHA or `@v5` doesn't reduce the rate, the issue is on the runner side (token rotation / DNS / cache) and requires a different mitigation — switch to SSH-based checkout via `ssh-key`. If THAT also doesn't fix it, file an issue against github.com/actions/checkout with the failure logs from PRs #738+ and escalate to GHE platform support.
  - **Measurement**: `node scripts/gh-actions-flake-tracker.mjs --window=20-prs --filter='actions/checkout' --json | jq '.failure_rate_per_job <= 0.005'` returns true; weekly METRICS.md entry shows the rate.
  - **Anchor**: rule #17 (proactive heal — observation IS the fix; this scout task is the same-session response to the flake); rule #4 (everything measurable, everything visible — a flake rate without a metric is invisible); rule #11 (no flaky metric is load-bearing — currently the rollup CI gate IS load-bearing AND the checkout flake makes it unreliable, which is a constitutional violation of rule #11 worth a dedicated fix); GitHub, "actions/checkout — Best practices for git authentication", docs.github.com (consult for the canonical mitigation list).
  - **Files**: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (the `uses: actions/checkout@v4` lines — 50+ jobs all reference this), `scripts/gh-actions-flake-tracker.mjs` (new), `METRICS.md` (new row), `vision.md` § "Pattern conformance index" (new row for the flake-tracker script).

- [ ] `lint-stack-audit-post-openhands-wrap` — audit + delete lint stages redundant with OpenHands' output; target 53 → 20 stages
  - **ID**: lint-stack-audit-post-openhands-wrap
  - **Tags**: p1, strategic, reshape, observed-2026-05-22, codebase-reduction, PROJ-needed
  - **Blocked**: needs-openhands-wrap-shipped (depends on `add-openhands-as-pluggable-backend` landing first)
  - **Milestone**: M2
  - **Competitive-goal**: closes the "70K LOC for 1 operator is unsustainable" math from the 2026-05-22 strategic review. The lint stack is 53K LOC (~75% of the codebase). Many stages exist because Minsky couldn't trust spawned agents (Claude/Devin/Aider) to do the right thing. Post-OpenHands-wrap, the agent has higher single-task quality (65.8% SWE-bench, critic + best-of-N) and inherits OpenHands' own quality gates. Many Minsky lint stages become redundant. Deleting them is the largest single lever for getting Minsky to the 10K-LOC sustainable footprint (Path C of the strategic review).
  - **Touches**: `scripts/run-pre-pr-lint-stack.mjs` (the orchestrator that lists the 53 stages), each `scripts/check-*.mjs` lint script that becomes redundant (delete + remove from the stack), `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (CI jobs that mirror local lints — delete in lockstep), `AGENTS.md` § rule references (some constitutional rules may need re-anchoring to surviving lints), `docs/PRACTICES.md` (the lint-stack documentation), `vision.md` rule #10 (deterministic enforcement — the surviving lint set must still cover the 17 constitutional rules with at least 1 lint each).
  - **Details**: 2026-05-22 strategic review (docs/strategic-review-2026-05-22-continue-or-wrap-more.md § "What's wrapped vs what stays Minsky") identified lint-stack pruning as the largest deletion target. Procedure: (a) for each of the 53 pre-pr-lint stages, write a 1-sentence "why does this exist?" justification, (b) check whether OpenHands' output already satisfies the invariant the lint guards against (e.g., `brief-pr-instructions` checks the PR body has a "## Why this is needed" section — OpenHands' PR generator likely produces this by default; verify), (c) for each redundant lint, delete the script + remove from the stack + remove the CI job, (d) for each non-redundant lint, document WHY it survives (so future audits don't re-question), (e) re-run pre-pr-lint --stage=full on 3 fixture PRs to confirm the slim stack catches the same violation set the full stack did. Target output: 53 → 20 stages, with a documented justification table for the surviving 20.
  - **Hypothesis**: at least 25 of the 53 pre-pr-lint stages are belt-and-braces for failure modes OpenHands' agent doesn't produce. Aggressive deletion saves ~25K LOC of script code + frees the operator from maintaining lints that catch zero violations in practice.
  - **Success**: `pnpm pre-pr-lint --stage=full` shows ≤20 stages green; LOC of `scripts/check-*.mjs` files drops by ≥25K; the constitutional-rule coverage (each of 17 rules → ≥1 deterministic lint) is preserved in the surviving stack; integration test runs the slim stack against the 10 most-recent merged PRs and confirms zero false-positives.
  - **Pivot**: if the audit reveals only ~5 stages are redundant, the deletion strategy is wrong — most lints catch real Minsky-specific violations that OpenHands doesn't address. Revisit Path C; consider whether constitution-as-CI is doing more work than the strategic review credited it for.
  - **Measurement**: `pnpm pre-pr-lint --stage=full 2>&1 | grep -c '\[ok\]'` returns ≤20; `wc -l scripts/check-*.mjs | tail -1` shows ≥25K LOC reduction vs baseline; `node scripts/audit-constitutional-rule-coverage.mjs` (new — verifies each rule has ≥1 lint).
  - **Anchor**: rule #1 (don't reinvent — OpenHands ships quality gates we'd otherwise build); rule #10 (deterministic enforcement — surviving lints must still cover all 17 rules); docs/strategic-review-2026-05-22-continue-or-wrap-more.md § "Path C — Continue, but RESHAPE"; competitors/openhands.md § "What we keep" — the wrap analysis explicitly noted Minsky owns the constitution layer; this task is the reshape of HOW Minsky owns it (fewer lints, sharper coverage).

- [ ] `novel-packages-audit-post-wrap` — audit + consolidate `novel/` packages; target 14 → 8
  - **ID**: novel-packages-audit-post-wrap
  - **Tags**: p1, strategic, reshape, observed-2026-05-22, codebase-reduction, PROJ-needed
  - **Blocked**: needs-openhands-wrap-shipped (depends on `add-openhands-as-pluggable-backend` landing first)
  - **Milestone**: M2
  - **Competitive-goal**: closes the "14 novel/ packages for 1 operator is too many" structural debt from the 2026-05-22 strategic review. Each novel/ package has its own README + test suite + dist build + tsconfig + dependency surface; the per-package fixed cost compounds across 14. Consolidating to ~8 packages reduces fixed cost by ~40% without losing functionality.
  - **Touches**: `novel/*/` (each candidate package — read README + dependents to confirm consolidation safety), `pnpm-workspace.yaml` (workspace declarations), `ARCHITECTURE.md` § "Dependency table" (the canonical list of novel/ packages — must stay in sync), `vision.md` § "Pattern conformance index" (any package that gets folded loses its index row + that row moves to the absorbing package).
  - **Details**: 2026-05-22 strategic review identified 14 novel/ packages as candidates for consolidation. Specific candidates: (a) `competitive-benchmark` → fold into `experiment-record` once OpenHands Index becomes the corpus harness (`research-finding-multi-task-benchmark-suite` already filed in TASKS.md P3); (b) `dashboard-web` → replace with `minsky watch` CLI subscribing to OpenHands' WebSocket (already planned in the wrap vision); (c) `mape-k-loop` → fold into `experiment-record` if user-story-003 stays in Specification phase past M2; (d) `spec-monitor` + `observer` → can these merge? Both observe runtime invariants. (e) `sidecar-bootstrap` → probably stays (operator-machine-identity-critical). For each merger, write a 2-paragraph "why the merger preserves the layered architecture" justification; reject mergers that violate rule #2 (every dependency through an interface). Target output: 14 → 8 packages, with a documented justification table.
  - **Hypothesis**: 6 of the 14 packages have meaningful overlap with another package OR with a planned OpenHands wrap. Consolidating saves ~10K LOC of fixed-cost infrastructure (per-package READMEs + tsconfig + dist build + transitive deps).
  - **Success**: `ls novel/ | wc -l` returns ≤8; `ARCHITECTURE.md` § "Dependency table" reflects the new structure; all integration tests still pass; the `rule-2-dep-coverage` lint exits 0.
  - **Pivot**: if the audit reveals all 14 packages have load-bearing distinct responsibilities (no clean mergers), the 14-package structure is correct — but then revisit whether each package's per-stage lints can be deleted instead (overlap with `lint-stack-audit-post-openhands-wrap`).
  - **Measurement**: `ls novel/ | wc -l` ≤8; `fd . novel/ -t d -d 1 | wc -l` matches; `pnpm exec vitest run` exits 0 across the new package boundaries.
  - **Anchor**: rule #1 (don't reinvent — fold what duplicates other capabilities); rule #2 (every dependency through an interface — merge-candidates must preserve interface boundaries); docs/strategic-review-2026-05-22-continue-or-wrap-more.md § "Path C — Continue, but RESHAPE"; ARCHITECTURE.md § "Dependency table" (the inventory of current packages).

- [ ] `readme-honest-3-developer-user-test` — MILESTONES.md M1.11 requires that 3 developers who've never seen Minsky can install and run it from clone to first iteration in <5min reading time, using only the README. No formal user test has been conducted yet — the criterion is currently `🟡 partial` based on README length alone.
  - **ID**: readme-honest-3-developer-user-test
  - **Tags**: p1, milestone-m1, m1-11, ux, install, user-test, readme, observed-2026-05-22
  - **Milestone**: M1
  - **Hypothesis**: README in <130 lines that follows the "agent-mediated install" or "manual clone + pnpm install + ./bin/minsky" path is *sufficient* for a developer to get from `git clone` to first iteration in <5min. Falsifiable: the test will surface friction points (missing prereqs, ambiguous instructions, undocumented dependencies, opinionated AI-agent assumptions) within the first 3 user runs.
  - **Success**: 3 developers (with varied backgrounds — at least one outside the operator's direct network) complete the install-to-first-iteration flow following only the README. Median time-to-first-iteration ≤ 5min. Zero blocking issues that require operator clarification mid-flow. Each developer files at least one improvement task (or marks zero tasks needed — which is itself a signal).
  - **Pivot**: if even ONE developer takes > 10min OR requires operator help OR can't finish, the README needs a structural fix (not a wording tweak). Threshold: 2 of 3 succeeding meets the bar; <2 of 3 fails M1.11 and the README gets rewritten on the friction-point findings.
  - **Measurement**: `node scripts/user-test-results.mjs --window=30d --json | jq '.successful_runs >= 3 && .median_time_minutes <= 5'` returns `true`. Until that script exists, an interim `docs/user-tests/2026-MM-DD-readme-test.md` file documenting each developer's run (timing, friction points, outcome) is the artefact.
  - **Anchor**: MILESTONES.md M1.11 acceptance ("User test: 3 developers who've never seen Minsky can install and run it"); Nielsen 1993 *Usability Engineering* (5 users uncover ~85% of usability issues; this task picks 3 to surface the most-likely blockers fast); rule #3 (test-first — the user test IS the validation of M1.11's claim).
  - **Details**: (a) Recruit 3 developers — pick from the operator's network OR via an internal slack channel — none should have run Minsky before. (b) Provide them with the README link and nothing else; observe via screenshare or async log. (c) Time from `git clone` to first iteration record in `.minsky/orchestrate.jsonl` (or first PR opened). (d) Capture friction in a per-developer `docs/user-tests/2026-MM-DD-<initials>.md`. (e) Aggregate findings into a "README improvement opportunities" list; file any > 5-min blockers as P1 tasks against the README. (f) Build `scripts/user-test-results.mjs` to parse the per-developer test files and emit the median-time + success-rate metric (the measurement command).
  - **Files**: `docs/user-tests/2026-MM-DD-<initials>.md` (new per-developer reports), `scripts/user-test-results.mjs` (new aggregator), `docs/user-tests/README.md` (new — explains the test methodology); per-developer P1 follow-ups against README friction points filed as separate tasks.
  - **Surfaced-by**: 2026-05-22 M1 status audit — every other M1.X criterion has either a passing measurement or a paired task. M1.11 has neither; this fills the gap.

- [ ] `m1-retag-v0-1-0-after-completion` — once `node scripts/m1-metrics-dashboard.mjs` shows 81/81 passing AND every M1.X status row in MILESTONES.md is `✅ done`, retag the M1-complete commit as `v0.1.0-m1` (or `v0.1.0` after a `git tag -d v0.2.0` cleanup) so the milestone tag matches the milestone reality.
  - **ID**: m1-retag-v0-1-0-after-completion
  - **Tags**: p1, milestone-m1, release-engineering, semver, observed-2026-05-22
  - **Milestone**: M1
  - **Hypothesis**: separating the *milestone* tag from the *semver* tag (`v0.1.0-m1` for the milestone, `v0.X.Y` for any semantic-release auto-bumps) makes the milestone progress legible without fighting `semantic-release`. The auto-bumps continue (`v0.2.0` was a `feat:` minor bump that pre-empted M2's reserved version); the milestone tag is a separate retroactive marker for "this commit cleared M1".
  - **Success**: a `v0.1.0-m1` annotated git tag exists on the commit where `pnpm m1:metrics` first reports 81/81 passing. The tag's annotation links to `docs/m1-completion-report.md`. README and MILESTONES.md cite this tag as the canonical "M1 shipped" marker.
  - **Pivot**: if `semantic-release` later auto-promotes to `v0.3.0` etc. before milestone tags can be applied, switch to a calendar-anchored tag scheme (`m1-2026-MM-DD-complete`) that's unambiguous about milestone-vs-version separation.
  - **Measurement**: `git tag --list 'v0.1.0-m1*' | wc -l` returns 1 once shipped. `pnpm m1:metrics --json | jq '.pass == .total'` returns `true` on the same commit.
  - **Anchor**: MILESTONES.md § "Versioning note" (operator directive 2026-05-22 — released tags do not auto-promote a milestone); Beck 2002 *Test-Driven Development*, Ch. 7 (test-driven version markers); rule #4 (everything measurable, everything visible — the milestone-complete state IS measurable, so the tag should reflect it).
  - **Details**: (a) Watch the M1 dashboard daily; when pass-count hits 81/81, snapshot the commit. (b) `docs/m1-completion-report.md` writes up the full M1 lap (what shipped, what stayed in scope, lessons). (c) `git tag -a v0.1.0-m1 <commit-sha> -m "M1 complete — see docs/m1-completion-report.md"`. (d) Push the tag. (e) Optionally `gh release create v0.1.0-m1 --notes-file docs/m1-completion-report.md` for the GH-Releases surface.
  - **Files**: `docs/m1-completion-report.md` (new); `MILESTONES.md` (M1 § gets a final `Shipped: <date>, see v0.1.0-m1` line).
  - **Surfaced-by**: 2026-05-22 docs sweep — the v0.2.0 auto-bump made the released-tag semantics confusing. Filing this as the durable shape so the operator doesn't have to fight semantic-release.

- [ ] `motivation-bullet-test-coverage-completion` — every motivation bullet in the README's "Why Minsky?" section has a sibling integration test file that drives the claimed behaviour end-to-end against fixture data; 4 stories shipped without tests in this PR (007, 008, 009, 010) need their `.test.ts` files
  - **ID**: motivation-bullet-test-coverage-completion
  - **Tags**: p1, milestone-m1, tests, user-stories, observability, observed-2026-05-20
  - **Milestone**: M1
  - **Surfaced-by**: operator 2026-05-20 reviewing the "Why Minsky?" section — "ensure each of those points is a user story with full integration/runtime test suite". Audit revealed 4 motivation bullets had stories landing in this PR but no sibling test files. The stories specify the test contract (`## Integration test` section with Setup/Action/Assert per story); this task writes the actual `.test.ts` files.
  - **Hypothesis**: each motivation bullet's user story names exactly one integration test file in its `## Integration test` section. Writing those 4 files closes the gap from "the story claims X works" to "X works under CI". Measured by the count of motivation-bullet integration tests passing in `pnpm test` (target = 7 — one per bullet); today = 3 (stories 001/003 ship tests; 004 ships story without a test; 007/008/009/010 ship stories in this PR without tests).
  - **Success**: 7/7 motivation bullets have a passing integration test in `user-stories/<NNN>-*.test.ts` covering at least the Setup/Action/Assert specified in the story. Each test file imports the real implementation (not just a stub) where the impl exists; where the impl is partial (008 multi-persona, 010 qa-log), the test uses `it.skip` guarded by an env flag that activates when the M2/P0 ships.
  - **Pivot**: if writing the integration tests reveals that the stories' Assert criteria are mis-specified (e.g., the daemon doesn't actually log `backend=<choice>` in the exact format claimed), update the STORY rather than the test — stories are specs, and a story that doesn't match the implementation is itself a defect. Pivot threshold: ≥2 of 7 stories require Assert revisions, indicating the audit was imprecise.
  - **Measurement**: `pnpm vitest run user-stories/00{4,7,8,9}-*.test.ts user-stories/010-*.test.ts --reporter=json | jq -e '.numFailedTests == 0 and .numTotalTests >= 30'` — script exits 0 only if all 4 new test files pass with at least 30 total cases across them.
  - **Anchor**: Beck 1999 *Extreme Programming Explained* Ch. 17 (CI as constraint enforcer — claims without tests are aspiration; tests are what make claims load-bearing). Composed with rule #10 (deterministic enforcement — every rule is a CI lint).
  - **Details**: Five test files to write. (1) `user-stories/004-budget-auto-pause.test.ts` — story exists, no test. Drive `claude-budget-guard` against fixture token-monitor data; assert pause-at-70%, downgrade-to-haiku, resume-on-window-reset. (2) `user-stories/007-cto-audit-files-new-tasks.test.ts` — drive `runHostCtoAudit` in dry-run mode against a synthetic host repo fixture; assert proposed tasks pass rule-9, duplicates skipped, priority bumps gated on 2+ observations. (3) `user-stories/008-per-task-backend-and-personas.test.ts` — drive `selectBackend` over 6 fixture tasks; assert each routing rule; the multi-persona test stays `it.skip` until M2 ships handoff-spec. (4) `user-stories/009-forced-research-rule-1.test.ts` — drive `scripts/check-rule-1-novel-justification.mjs --fixture <dir>` against good-package/bad-package/placeholder-package fixtures; assert exit codes + stderr substrings. (5) `user-stories/010-async-human-qa-via-file.test.ts` — drive `askHuman` API against tmp-dir fixture with synchronous Q/A simulation via fake-timers; the test starts `describe.skipIf(!process.env.MINSKY_QA_LOG_ENABLED)` and activates when P0 `minsky-human-comm-via-file` ships. Also expand `user-stories/001-loop-runs-overnight.test.ts` from 1 test to ≥5 (current state is thin — story acceptance criteria has 6 items, tests have 1).
  - **Files**: `user-stories/004-budget-auto-pause.test.ts` (new), `user-stories/007-cto-audit-files-new-tasks.test.ts` (new), `user-stories/008-per-task-backend-and-personas.test.ts` (new), `user-stories/009-forced-research-rule-1.test.ts` (new), `user-stories/010-async-human-qa-via-file.test.ts` (new with skipped describe), `user-stories/001-loop-runs-overnight.test.ts` (expand), `test/fixtures/rule-1/good-package/` (new), `test/fixtures/rule-1/bad-package/` (new), `test/fixtures/rule-1/placeholder-package/` (new), `test/fixtures/cto-audit/host-repo/` (new).
  - **Touches**: user-stories/, test/fixtures/, novel/cross-repo-runner/src/host-cto-audit.ts (export a dry-run mode if not already), novel/tick-loop/src/llm-provider-selector.ts (export `selectBackend` if not already).
  - **Acceptance**: (1) The Measurement command above exits 0. (2) `pnpm pre-pr-lint --stage=full` passes — the new fixtures don't trip any other linter (rule-1 grandfathering, rule-7 chaos coverage). (3) The 5 new tests run in <30s wall-clock combined (CI cost budget). (4) `user-stories/001-loop-runs-overnight.test.ts` has ≥5 `it()` blocks covering the 6 acceptance criteria.
  - **Risk**: low. The implementations for 004/007/008/009 exist; tests just exercise them. 010's test is `describe.skipIf` until the P0 ships. The risk is mostly fixture authoring time + occasional Assert-revision iteration with the story author.
  - **Note**: composes with P0 `minsky-human-comm-via-file` (the implementation for story 010's tests to activate against), the M2 multi-persona pipeline (story 008's skipped pipeline test activates then), and the existing rule-9 / rule-3-doc-first gates (every new test file ships with its story already documented).

- [ ] `cto-audit-merge-rate-metric` — the CTO-audit user story 007 declares a secondary metric `cto_audit_filed_tasks_merged_within_30d` (the merge rate of audit-filed tasks) but no aggregator exists today; this task ships the metric so the story's quality threshold (≥40% merge rate) is verifiable
  - **ID**: cto-audit-merge-rate-metric
  - **Tags**: p1, milestone-m1, observability, metrics, user-stories
  - **Milestone**: M1
  - **Surfaced-by**: operator 2026-05-20 — drafting user-story 007 surfaced the secondary metric. Without it, the audit's quality is unmeasurable; without measurement, rule #11 forbids the quality claim from being load-bearing.
  - **Hypothesis**: a per-week aggregator that joins TASKS.md `**Surfaced-by**: daemon CTO audit` entries with `gh pr list --search "<task-id> in:title"` (merged status + closedAt) computes `cto_audit_filed_tasks_merged_within_30d` as a percentage. Storing as `metric-snapshots/cto-audit-merge-rate.jsonl` lets the dashboard chart trend.
  - **Success**: weekly aggregator emits `{ts, audit_filed_count, merged_within_30d_count, merge_rate}` per host; the dashboard's CTO-audit tile renders the merge rate; rule #11 flake-detection lint doesn't trip on this metric (i.e., the metric is deterministic given the same inputs).
  - **Pivot**: if the merge-rate metric proves noisy (varies >20% week-over-week with no underlying change), drop to a 90-day rolling window or use a quarter as the unit. Threshold: 4 consecutive weeks with rolling-week stddev >20% of mean.
  - **Measurement**: `node scripts/aggregate-cto-audit-metrics.mjs --since=2026-05-01 --until=2026-05-31 | jq -e '.merge_rate >= 0.4'` — exits 0 only when the merge-rate hits the user-story-007 threshold.
  - **Anchor**: rule #11 (vision.md § 11 — no flaky metric is load-bearing; metrics must be deterministic) composed with rule #4 (vision.md § 4 — everything measurable, everything visible).
  - **Details**: `scripts/aggregate-cto-audit-metrics.mjs` (new) — reads TASKS.md committed history (`git log --all -- TASKS.md`), greps for `**Surfaced-by**: daemon CTO audit`, joins with `gh pr list --json number,title,state,mergedAt` matching task IDs by title, computes merge rate over 30-day windows. Output is a JSONL stream consumed by the dashboard's `metric-snapshots` ingestion.
  - **Files**: `scripts/aggregate-cto-audit-metrics.mjs` (new), `scripts/aggregate-cto-audit-metrics.test.mjs` (new — paired test with synthetic git+gh fixtures), `METRICS.md` (add a section).
  - **Acceptance**: Measurement command runs; the script's tests pass; the dashboard renders the tile.
  - **Risk**: low. Pure aggregation over data sources that already exist (git history + gh CLI).

- [ ] `multi-persona-pipeline-handoff-spec` — finalise `novel/handoff-spec/` (the JSON schema + validator + writer + reader for persona handoff files) so the M2 multi-persona pipeline can spawn researcher → planner → developer → QA → reviewer on a single task; user-story 008's `it.skip` cases activate when this ships
  - **ID**: multi-persona-pipeline-handoff-spec
  - **Tags**: p1, milestone-m2, multi-persona, novel-package, handoffs
  - **Milestone**: M2
  - **Surfaced-by**: operator 2026-05-20 — drafting user-story 008 surfaced the M2 path's first missing piece. The story documents the experience; this task ships the substrate.
  - **Hypothesis**: a per-persona handoff file at `.minsky/handoffs/<task-id>/<persona>-<iso-ts>.md` with a JSON-Schema-validated header (`{persona, taskId, status: 'done'|'failed'|'in-progress', producedAt, nextPersona, attachments?}`) plus free-form Markdown body is sufficient for the pipeline. Each persona writes its handoff on exit; the next persona reads it on start. Validation at the schema level catches the most common bug (persona writes the wrong nextPersona or omits status).
  - **Success**: a 5-persona pipeline (researcher → planner → developer → QA → reviewer) runs end-to-end against a single fixture task; each handoff file validates against the schema; the pipeline ends with a complete reviewer-produced PR description. Pipeline-completion rate ≥80% over the first 20 production runs.
  - **Pivot**: if 5 personas prove too many (overhead dominates per-task progress), drop to 3 (planner → developer → reviewer) and re-baseline. Threshold: median per-task wall-clock exceeds 60 min on first 10 prod runs.
  - **Measurement**: `node scripts/measure-pipeline.mjs --runs=20 --task-fixture=test/fixtures/pipeline/complex-task.md | jq -e '.completion_rate >= 0.8 and .median_wall_clock_minutes <= 60'`.
  - **Anchor**: Hewitt 1973 Actor Model (IJCAI — independent actors communicate via messages) composed with Hayes-Roth 1985 Blackboard (specialists writing to a shared workspace). The handoff file IS the message; the blackboard IS the `.minsky/handoffs/<task-id>/` directory.
  - **Details**: (1) Finalise the JSON schema at `novel/handoff-spec/schema.json`. (2) Ship `novel/handoff-spec/src/{validate,write,read}.ts` with paired tests. (3) Wire into `novel/tick-loop/src/spawn-strategy.ts` so pipeline-tagged tasks fan out the personas. (4) Per-persona budget caps in config. (5) Failure mode: if any persona fails, the pipeline files `pipeline-failed-<task>-<persona>` and stops; the operator unblocks manually.
  - **Files**: `novel/handoff-spec/schema.json` (new), `novel/handoff-spec/src/{validate,write,read}.ts` (new), sibling tests, `novel/tick-loop/src/spawn-strategy.ts` (wire pipeline branch), `user-stories/008-per-task-backend-and-personas.test.ts` (activate the skipped pipeline test).
  - **Touches**: novel/handoff-spec/, novel/tick-loop/src/spawn-strategy.ts, user-stories/008-*.test.ts
  - **Acceptance**: pipeline completes for ≥80% of 20 fixture runs; per-persona handoff files validate; the reviewer-produced PR description is non-empty and references the prior personas' outputs.
  - **Risk**: medium. Multi-persona introduces handoff-spec brittleness (each persona's idea of "done" may differ). Mitigation: the schema's `status` enum is the contract; an invalid status fails fast.
  - **Note**: blocked on nothing; this is M2 net-new work. The user-story 008's `it.skip` test activates against this implementation.

- [ ] `claude-agents-deep-integration` — minsky's persona library (researcher, manager, developer, qa-engineer, reviewer, alternatives-analyst, indie-hacker, growth-hacker, strategist, agent-improver, user, pipeline-runner) is exposed as Claude Code sub-agents under `.claude/agents/*.md` so the operator can invoke them via `/agents` AND the M2 multi-persona pipeline executes through Claude's native sub-agent surface instead of shelling out to a separate CLI subprocess per persona
  - **ID**: claude-agents-deep-integration
  - **Tags**: p1, milestone-m2, integration, claude, personas, handoffs, observed-2026-05-20
  - **Milestone**: M2
  - **Surfaced-by**: operator 2026-05-20 — "Let's ensure that deep integration with claude's /agents command is P1 task". Today minsky ships personas as `.claude/skills/orchestrator-*` markdown (operator-facing skills the human can read) AND as `subagent_*` profiles in the global agentbrew (the `subagent_explore` / `subagent_general` profiles I use via `run_subagent`). Neither is the Claude Code `.claude/agents/*.md` sub-agent format — so the operator cannot type `/agents` in their Claude session and see minsky's persona library. The personas exist conceptually in three formats but none are the canonical one Claude Code reads.
  - **Hypothesis**: Claude Code reads sub-agent definitions from `.claude/agents/*.md` with YAML frontmatter (`name`, `description`, `tools`, `model`). Generating these files from the existing `.claude/skills/orchestrator-*/SKILL.md` source (which already encodes the persona's identity, scope, tool-access posture, and trigger phrases) is a deterministic transformation.
